 `./claude/resources/tasks/05-cross-cutting.md`

```text
1	# Phase 5: Cross-cutting Concerns (Client-focused)
2	
3	These are shared infrastructure components that multiple client-facing pages depend on. They enforce consistency across the public surface and reduce duplication.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 5.1 Admin Route Auth Guard Middleware
8	
9	**Description:** Create a Next.js middleware that protects all `/admin/*` routes. Unauthenticated requests redirect to a login page. Uses NextAuth.js session check.
10	
11	**Acceptance criteria:**
12	- [ ] All routes under `/admin` require valid session
13	- [ ] Unauthenticated users are redirected to `/admin/login`
14	- [ ] Session check happens server-side via NextAuth middleware
15	- [ ] Authenticated users proceed normally
16	- [ ] Login page is excluded from the guard (no redirect loop)
17	- [ ] API routes under `/api/admin/*` return 401 JSON for unauthenticated requests
18	
19	**Technical notes:**
20	- Use Next.js `middleware.ts` with `matcher` config targeting `/admin/:path*`
21	- Check session via `getToken()` from `next-auth/jwt`
22	- Keep middleware lightweight; no DB calls, JWT check only
23	- API routes should return `{ error: "Tidak dibenarkan" }` with 401 status
24	
25	**Dependencies:** 1.x (Foundation — NextAuth setup)
26	**Estimate:** S
27	
28	---
29	
30	## 5.2 Global Error Boundary for Client Pages
31	
32	**Description:** Wrap all public pages in an error boundary that catches rendering errors and displays a branded fallback UI instead of a white screen. Server errors during SSR should also render a styled error page.
33	
34	**Acceptance criteria:**
35	- [ ] Runtime errors in client pages show a branded error screen
36	- [ ] Error screen matches MawarBiru luxury aesthetic (navy/cream palette)
37	- [ ] Error screen includes: apologetic message in Bahasa Malaysia, "Kembali ke Laman Utama" CTA
38	- [ ] `error.tsx` file exists at the app route level
39	- [ ] `global-error.tsx` exists for root layout failures
40	- [ ] Errors are logged to console (monitoring hook point for later)
41	- [ ] Error boundary does not affect admin pages (admin has own error handling)
42	
43	**States:**
44	- Default: Normal rendering, boundary invisible
45	- Error: Branded fallback with message + CTA button
46	- Hover/Focus on CTA: Standard button states from design system
47	
48	**Responsive:**
49	- Mobile: Centered layout, stacked content, full-width CTA
50	- Tablet/Desktop: Centered card with constrained max-width
51	
52	**Accessibility:**
53	- Focus moves to the error heading when boundary activates
54	- CTA is keyboard accessible
55	- Screen reader announces error context
56	
57	**Technical notes:**
58	- Use Next.js App Router `error.tsx` convention
59	- Include a `reset()` callback button to retry rendering
60	- Style with Tailwind using brand tokens from Phase 2
61	- Keep error page self-contained (no external data fetching)
62	
63	**Dependencies:** 2.x (Design system — Button, typography tokens)
64	**Estimate:** S
65	**Out of scope:** Error tracking/monitoring service integration (Phase 8)
66	
67	---
68	
69	## 5.3 Loading States and Skeleton Loaders
70	
71	**Description:** Create reusable loading components: a full-page skeleton loader, inline skeleton primitives (text lines, cards, calendar grid), and a branded spinner. These are used across Temujanji, Kalendar, and layout transitions.
72	
73	**Acceptance criteria:**
74	- [ ] `<SkeletonText>` component renders animated placeholder lines (configurable count)
75	- [ ] `<SkeletonCard>` renders a card-shaped placeholder matching the slot card design
76	- [ ] `<SkeletonCalendar>` renders a calendar-grid-shaped placeholder for `/kalendar`
77	- [ ] `<LoadingSpinner>` renders a branded spinner using MawarBiru palette
78	- [ ] `loading.tsx` files exist for `/temujanji` and `/kalendar` routes
79	- [ ] Skeleton pulse animation uses `#EBDED4` (cream) to `#D9AA90` (dusty rose) gradient
80	- [ ] Animation respects `prefers-reduced-motion` — falls back to static placeholder
81	
82	**States:**
83	- Loading: Animated pulse/shimmer
84	- Reduced motion: Static grey placeholders, no animation
85	
86	**Responsive:**
87	- Skeletons match the responsive layout of the content they replace
88	- Mobile: Single-column skeletons
89	- Desktop: Multi-column where the real content is multi-column
90	
91	**Accessibility:**
92	- Skeleton containers have `aria-busy="true"` and `role="status"`
93	- Screen reader: `aria-label="Memuatkan..."` on the container
94	
95	**Technical notes:**
96	- Use Tailwind `animate-pulse` as base, customize with brand colours
97	- Export from a shared `components/ui/skeleton/` directory
98	- `loading.tsx` files leverage Next.js App Router Suspense integration automatically
99	
100	**Dependencies:** 2.x (Design system tokens)
101	**Estimate:** M
102	**Out of scope:** Admin-side loading states (handled within admin components)
103	
104	---
105	
106	## 5.4 Empty State Components
107	
108	**Description:** Create branded empty state displays for when data-driven pages have no content. Primary consumers: Temujanji page (no available slots), Kalendar (no events in selected month).
109	
110	**Acceptance criteria:**
111	- [ ] `<EmptyState>` component accepts: icon (optional), title, description, CTA (optional)
112	- [ ] Temujanji empty state: "Tiada slot tersedia" with suggestion to check back later or contact via WhatsApp
113	- [ ] Kalendar empty state: "Tiada acara pada bulan ini" with month navigation hint
114	- [ ] Styled with MawarBiru palette — subtle, not alarming
115	- [ ] Each empty state includes an optional WhatsApp CTA link
116	
117	**States:**
118	- Default: Centered illustration/icon + text + optional CTA
119	- Hover/Focus on CTA: Standard button hover from design system
120	
121	**Responsive:**
122	- Mobile: Full-width centered, comfortable padding
123	- Desktop: Constrained max-width, centered in content area
124	
125	**Accessibility:**
126	- Empty state region has `role="status"` so screen readers announce it
127	- CTA is keyboard navigable
128	
129	**Technical notes:**
130	- Generic `<EmptyState>` component; page-specific instances pass props
131	- Use a simple SVG icon or emoji placeholder (luxury-appropriate)
132	- Place in `components/ui/empty-state/`
133	
134	**Dependencies:** 2.x (Design system — typography, Button)
135	**Estimate:** S
136	**Out of scope:** Admin empty states (use basic "Tiada rekod" text)
137	
138	---
139	
140	## 5.5 Toast Notification System
141	
142	**Description:** Implement a toast notification system for success/error feedback on the Temujanji booking form and admin actions. Toasts appear at the top or bottom of the viewport, auto-dismiss after a timeout, and are dismissible manually.
143	
144	**Acceptance criteria:**
145	- [ ] `<ToastProvider>` wraps the app and manages toast state
146	- [ ] `useToast()` hook exposes `showToast({ type, message, duration? })` function
147	- [ ] Toast types: `success` (green/dusty-rose), `error` (terracotta/red), `info` (navy)
148	- [ ] Toasts auto-dismiss after 5 seconds (configurable)
149	- [ ] Toasts can be manually dismissed with close button
150	- [ ] Multiple toasts stack vertically
151	- [ ] Toast entrance/exit has slide + fade animation
152	- [ ] Animation respects `prefers-reduced-motion`
153	
154	**States:**
155	- Hidden: No toast visible
156	- Visible: Toast slides in with message
157	- Hover on toast: Pause auto-dismiss timer
158	- Dismissing: Slide + fade out
159	
160	**Responsive:**
161	- Mobile: Full-width toast at bottom of viewport
162	- Desktop: Fixed-width toast at top-right corner
163	
164	**Accessibility:**
165	- Toast container has `role="alert"` and `aria-live="polite"`
166	- Close button has `aria-label="Tutup"`
167	- Keyboard: close button focusable, Escape dismisses topmost toast
168	
169	**Technical notes:**
170	- Use React Context + `useReducer` for toast state management
171	- Consider `sonner` or `react-hot-toast` as lightweight alternatives to custom implementation
172	- If using a library, ensure it supports custom styling with Tailwind
173	- Place provider in root layout
174	
175	**Dependencies:** 2.x (Design system tokens)
176	**Estimate:** M
177	**Out of scope:** Admin gets the same toast system (shared), but no additional admin-specific toast types
178	
179	---
180	
181	## 5.6 Temujanji Form Validation Pattern
182	
183	**Description:** Implement client-side and server-side validation for the Temujanji booking form. This establishes the validation pattern used across the app.
184	
185	**Acceptance criteria:**
186	- [ ] Client-side validation using Zod schema
187	- [ ] Fields validated: Nama (required, min 2 chars, max 100), No. Telefon (required, Malaysian format), Nota (optional, max 500 chars)
188	- [ ] Malaysian phone validation: accepts `01x-xxxxxxx` patterns (with or without dashes, with or without `+60` prefix)
189	- [ ] Inline error messages appear below each field in Bahasa Malaysia
190	- [ ] Errors display on blur and on submit attempt
191	- [ ] Submit button is disabled during submission (loading state)
192	- [ ] Server-side validation mirrors client-side Zod schema (shared schema file)
193	- [ ] API returns structured error response: `{ errors: { field: string[] } }`
194	
195	**States:**
196	- Default: Empty form, no errors shown
197	- Touched + invalid: Inline error below field, field border turns terracotta (`#A65E46`)
198	- Touched + valid: Field border returns to default (or subtle green check)
199	- Submitting: Button shows spinner, all fields disabled
200	- Success: Toast notification, form resets or redirects
201	- Server error: Toast with error message, form remains filled
202	
203	**Responsive:**
204	- Mobile: Stacked fields, full-width inputs
205	- Tablet/Desktop: Same stacked layout (form doesn't need multi-column)
206	
207	**Accessibility:**
208	- Error messages linked to fields via `aria-describedby`
209	- Invalid fields have `aria-invalid="true"`
210	- Focus moves to first error field on failed submit
211	- Error summary announced to screen readers
212	
213	**Technical notes:**
214	- Create shared Zod schema in `lib/validations/temujanji.ts`
215	- Use `react-hook-form` with `@hookform/resolvers/zod` for client-side
216	- Server action or API route reuses same Zod schema
217	- Phone regex: `/^(\+?60)?0?1[0-9]-?[0-9]{7,8}$/`
218	
219	**Dependencies:** 2.x (Design system — Input, Button), 4.x (Temujanji feature slice — form structure)
220	**Estimate:** M
221	**Out of scope:** SMS/OTP verification, email validation
222	
223	---
224	
225	## 5.7 WhatsApp Floating Button Component
226	
227	**Description:** Build a floating WhatsApp button that appears on all public pages. It links to the business owner's WhatsApp number via `wa.me` URL. Persistent across page navigations, visually prominent but not obstructing content.
228	
229	**Acceptance criteria:**
230	- [ ] Floating circular button fixed to bottom-right corner of viewport
231	- [ ] WhatsApp green icon (SVG) on the button
232	- [ ] Clicking opens `https://wa.me/<configured-number>` in new tab
233	- [ ] WhatsApp number is configurable via environment variable (`NEXT_PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER`)
234	- [ ] Button has subtle entrance animation on page load (fade + slide up)
235	- [ ] Button has hover scale effect
236	- [ ] Button does not overlap page footer when scrolled to bottom (offset margin)
237	- [ ] Button is visible on all public pages (`/`, `/temujanji`, `/kalendar`, `/pakej`)
238	- [ ] Button is NOT visible on admin pages
239	
240	**States:**
241	- Default: Green circular button with WhatsApp icon, slight shadow
242	- Hover: Scale up to 1.1x, shadow intensifies
243	- Focus: Visible focus ring (for keyboard users)
244	- Active: Scale down to 0.95x (press effect)
245	
246	**Responsive:**
247	- Mobile: 48x48px minimum touch target, 16px from edges
248	- Tablet: 56x56px, 24px from edges
249	- Desktop: 56x56px, 32px from edges
250	
251	**Accessibility:**
252	- `aria-label="Hubungi kami melalui WhatsApp"`
253	- `target="_blank"` with `rel="noopener noreferrer"`
254	- Keyboard focusable (naturally, as an `<a>` tag)
255	- Respects `prefers-reduced-motion` for entrance animation
256	
257	**Technical notes:**
258	- Implement as a client component in `components/ui/whatsapp-button.tsx`
259	- Include in the public layout (not root layout, to exclude admin)
260	- Use inline SVG for the WhatsApp icon (avoid external dependency)
261	- `z-index` should be below modals but above page content (e.g., `z-40`)
262	
263	**Dependencies:** 2.x (Design system tokens)
264	**Estimate:** S
265	**Out of scope:** WhatsApp chat widget, pre-filled message templates
266
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# MawarBiru — Task List
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	MawarBiru is a luxury wedding and event venue website built as a Next.js monolith with MySQL, Prisma, and Tailwind CSS. Public visitors browse packages, book consultation appointments (temujanji), and check venue availability via a colour-coded calendar. A single business owner manages everything through an admin panel.
6	
7	**Rigor split:** Client-facing pages (phases 3-4) receive full treatment — every interactive state, responsive breakpoint, accessibility requirement, edge case, and performance consideration is specified. Admin modules (phase 6) use standard rigor — functional correctness and basic usability without exhaustive state/a11y enumeration.
8	
9	## Dependency Overview
10	
11	```
12	Phase 1: Foundation
13	  └── Phase 2: Design System (depends on 1.1-1.4)
14	        └── Phase 3: Client Layout (depends on 2.1-2.5)
15	              └── Phase 4: Client Feature Slices (depends on 3.1-3.3)
16	                    └── Phase 5: Cross-cutting Concerns (depends on 2.x, 4.x)
17	                          └── Phase 6: Admin Surface (depends on 1.x, 2.x)
18	                                └── Phase 7: Quality & Polish (depends on all above)
19	                                      └── Phase 8: Release Prep (depends on all above)
20	```
21	
22	Phase 1 establishes the repo, database, and auth. Phase 2 builds the reusable design tokens and components. Phase 3 assembles the public-facing shell (nav, footer, layout). Phase 4 implements the four client pages with full interactivity. Phase 5 adds cross-cutting client concerns. Phase 6 builds the admin CRUD surface. Phase 7 polishes and audits. Phase 8 ships.
23	
24	## File Index
25	
26	| File | Phase | Surface | Tasks |
27	|------|-------|---------|-------|
28	| `01-foundation.md` | 1 — Foundation | Shared | 1.1–1.6 |
29	| `02-design-system.md` | 2 — Design System | Client | 2.1–2.5 |
30	| `03-client-layout.md` | 3 — Client Layout | Client | 3.1–3.3 |
31	| `04-client-homepage.md` | 4.1 — Homepage | Client | 4.1 |
32	| `04-client-pakej.md` | 4.2 — Pakej Page | Client | 4.2 |
33	| `04-client-temujanji.md` | 4.3, 4.5 — Temujanji | Client | 4.3, 4.5 |
34	| `04-client-kalendar.md` | 4.4, 4.6 — Kalendar | Client | 4.4, 4.6 |
35	| `05-cross-cutting.md` | 5 — Cross-cutting | Client | 5.1–5.7 |
36	| `06-admin-surface.md` | 6 — Admin Surface | Admin | 6.1–6.9 |
37	| `07-quality-polish.md` | 7 — Quality & Polish | Both | 7.1–7.5 |
38	| `08-release-prep.md` | 8 — Release Prep | Shared | 8.1–8.6 |
39	| `09-assumptions-and-questions.md` | — | — | — |
40
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./middleware.ts`

```text
1	import { auth } from "@/lib/auth";
2	import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
3	
4	export default auth((req) => {
5	  const { pathname } = req.nextUrl;
6	
7	  // Allow access to the login page without authentication
8	  if (pathname === "/admin/login") {
9	    return NextResponse.next();
10	  }
11	
12	  // Protect all other /admin/* routes
13	  if (!req.auth) {
14	    const loginUrl = new URL("/admin/login", req.nextUrl.origin);
15	    loginUrl.searchParams.set("callbackUrl", pathname);
16	    return NextResponse.redirect(loginUrl);
17	  }
18	
19	  return NextResponse.next();
20	});
21	
22	export const config = {
23	  matcher: ["/admin/:path*"],
24	};
25
```
</details></tool-use>

1	'use client';
2	
3	import { Toaster as SonnerToaster, toast } from 'sonner';
4	import { useEffect, useState } from 'react';
5	
6	type ToasterPosition =
7	  | 'top-left'
8	  | 'top-right'
9	  | 'bottom-left'
10	  | 'bottom-right'
11	  | 'top-center'
12	  | 'bottom-center';
13	
14	function useResponsivePosition(): ToasterPosition {
15	  const [position, setPosition] = useState<ToasterPosition>('bottom-right');
16	
17	  useEffect(() => {
18	    const mql = window.matchMedia('(min-width: 640px)');
19	
20	    function update(e: MediaQueryList | MediaQueryListEvent) {
21	      setPosition(e.matches ? 'bottom-right' : 'bottom-center');
22	    }
23	
24	    update(mql);
25	    mql.addEventListener('change', update);
26	    return () => mql.removeEventListener('change', update);
27	  }, []);
28	
29	  return position;
30	}
31	
32	function Toaster() {
33	  const position = useResponsivePosition();
34	
35	  return (
36	    <SonnerToaster
37	      position={position}
38	      toastOptions={{
39	        style: {
40	          backgroundColor: 'var(--color-cream)',
41	          color: 'var(--color-navy)',
42	          borderRadius: 'var(--radius-card)',
43	          boxShadow: 'var(--shadow-elevated)',
44	          border: '1px solid var(--color-cream-dark)',
45	          fontFamily: 'var(--font-sans)',
46	        },
47	      }}
48	    />
49	  );
50	}
51	
52	export { Toaster, toast };
53
```

1	import { forwardRef, type HTMLAttributes } from 'react';

3	import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';

5	const skeletonVariants = cva('animate-pulse bg-navy/10', {
6	  variants: {
7	    variant: {
8	      text: 'h-4 w-full rounded',
9	      circle: 'rounded-full',
10	      rect: 'rounded-card',
11	    },
12	  },
13	  defaultVariants: {
14	    variant: 'text',
15	  },
16	});
17	
18	type SkeletonProps = HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> &
19	  VariantProps<typeof skeletonVariants> & {
20	    'data-testid'?: string;
21	  };
22	
23	const Skeleton = forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, SkeletonProps>(
24	  ({ className, variant, ...props }, ref) => {
25	    return (
26	      <div
27	        ref={ref}
28	        aria-hidden="true"
29	        className={cn(skeletonVariants({ variant }), className)}
30	        {...props}
31	      />
32	    );
33	  },
34	);
35	
36	Skeleton.displayName = 'Skeleton';
37	
38	export { Skeleton, skeletonVariants };
39	export type { SkeletonProps };

1	'use client';
2	
3	import { type ReactNode } from 'react';
4	import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';
5	
6	interface EmptyStateAction {
7	  label: string;
8	  onClick: () => void;
9	}
10	
11	interface EmptyStateProps {
12	  title: string;
13	  description?: string;
14	  icon?: ReactNode;
15	  action?: EmptyStateAction;
16	  className?: string;
17	  'data-testid'?: string;
18	}
19	
20	function EmptyState({
21	  title,
22	  description,
23	  icon,
24	  action,
25	  className,
26	  ...props
27	}: EmptyStateProps) {
28	  return (
29	    <div
30	      className={cn(
31	        'flex flex-col items-center justify-center py-12 px-6 text-center',
32	        className,
33	      )}
34	      data-testid={props['data-testid']}
35	    >
36	      {icon && (
37	        <div className="mb-4 text-navy/30" aria-hidden="true">
38	          {icon}
39	        </div>
40	      )}
41	      <h3 className="font-serif text-lg font-semibold text-navy/70 mb-1">
42	        {title}
43	      </h3>
44	      {description && (
45	        <p className="text-sm text-navy/50 max-w-sm mb-6">{description}</p>
46	      )}
47	      {action && (
48	        <button
49	          type="button"
50	          onClick={action.onClick}
51	          className={cn(
52	            'inline-flex items-center justify-center',
53	            'h-10 px-4 text-sm font-medium',
54	            'rounded-button',
55	            'bg-navy text-cream hover:bg-navy-light active:bg-navy-dark',
56	            'transition-colors duration-150 motion-reduce:transition-none',
57	            'focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2 focus-visible:ring-dusty-rose',
58	          )}
59	        >
60	          {action.label}
61	        </button>
62	      )}
63	    </div>
64	  );
65	}
66	
67	export { EmptyState };
68	export type { EmptyStateProps, EmptyStateAction };
69

6	  variants: {
7	    size: {
8	      sm: 'h-4 w-4',
9	      md: 'h-6 w-6',
10	      lg: 'h-8 w-8',
11	    },
12	  },
13	  defaultVariants: {
14	    size: 'md',
15	  },
16	});
17	
18	type SpinnerProps = Omit<SVGAttributes<SVGSVGElement>, 'children'> &
19	  VariantProps<typeof spinnerVariants> & {
20	    'data-testid'?: string;
21	  };
22	
23	const Spinner = forwardRef<SVGSVGElement, SpinnerProps>(
24	  ({ className, size, ...props }, ref) => {
25	    return (
26	      <span role="status" aria-label="Memuatkan">
27	        <svg
28	          ref={ref}
29	          xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
30	          fill="none"
31	          viewBox="0 0 24 24"
32	          className={cn(spinnerVariants({ size }), className)}
33	          aria-hidden="true"
34	          {...props}
35	        >
36	          <circle
37	            className="opacity-25"
38	            cx="12"
39	            cy="12"
40	            r="10"
41	            stroke="currentColor"
42	            strokeWidth="4"
43	          />
44	          <path
45	            className="opacity-75"
46	            fill="currentColor"
47	            d="M4 12a8 8 0 018-8V0C5.373 0 0 5.373 0 12h4z"
48	          />
49	        </svg>
50	        <span className="sr-only">Memuatkan</span>
51	      </span>
52	    );
53	  },
54	);
55	
56	Spinner.displayName = 'Spinner';
57	
58	export { Spinner, spinnerVariants };
59	export type { SpinnerProps };
60
```

5	    .string({ error: 'Nama diperlukan' })
6	    .min(2, 'Nama mestilah sekurang-kurangnya 2 aksara')
7	    .max(100, 'Nama mestilah tidak melebihi 100 aksara'),

11	      /^01[0-9]-?\d{7,8}$/,
12	      'Sila masukkan nombor telefon Malaysia yang sah (cth: 0121234567)',
13	    ),
14	  nota: z
15	    .string()
16	    .max(500, 'Nota mestilah tidak melebihi 500 aksara')
17	    .optional(),
18	  slotTemujanjiId: z.string({ error: 'Slot temujanji diperlukan' }),
19	});
20	
21	export type TemujanjiInput = z.infer<typeof temujanjiSchema>;
22

143	    transform: translateY(100%);
144	  }
145	  to {

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-20 09:45:37Z)**_

Now I have a clear picture. Here's what's done vs needed:

- **5.1** Middleware exists but missing API route 401 JSON response for `/api/admin/*`
- **5.2** Missing: `error.tsx` and `global-error.tsx`
- **5.3** Base `Skeleton`/`Spinner` exist but missing: `SkeletonText`, `SkeletonCard`, `SkeletonCalendar`, `loading.tsx` files
- **5.4** `EmptyState` exists but missing `role="status"`, WhatsApp CTA link
- **5.5** Toast via sonner is done ✓
- **5.6** Zod schema exists but missing `+60` prefix support in regex
- **5.7** WhatsApp FAB is done ✓

4	related:
5	  title: Learn more about error handling
6	  links:
7	    - app/getting-started/error-handling
8	---
9	
10	An **error** file allows you to handle unexpected runtime errors and display fallback UI.
11	
12	<Image
13	  alt="error.js special file"
14	  srcLight="/docs/light/error-special-file.png"
15	  srcDark="/docs/dark/error-special-file.png"
16	  width="1600"
17	  height="606"
18	/>
19	
20	```tsx filename="app/dashboard/error.tsx" switcher
21	'use client' // Error boundaries must be Client Components
22	
23	import { useEffect } from 'react'
24	
25	export default function Error({
26	  error,
27	  unstable_retry,
28	}: {
29	  error: Error & { digest?: string }
30	  unstable_retry: () => void
31	}) {
32	  useEffect(() => {
33	    // Log the error to an error reporting service
34	    console.error(error)
35	  }, [error])
36	
37	  return (
38	    <div>
39	      <h2>Something went wrong!</h2>
40	      <button
41	        onClick={
42	          // Attempt to recover by re-fetching and re-rendering the segment
43	          () => unstable_retry()
44	        }
45	      >
46	        Try again
47	      </button>
48	    </div>
49	  )
50	}
51	```
52	
53	```jsx filename="app/dashboard/error.js" switcher
54	'use client' // Error boundaries must be Client Components
55	
56	import { useEffect } from 'react'
57	
58	export default function Error({ error, unstable_retry }) {
59	  useEffect(() => {
60	    // Log the error to an error reporting service
61	    console.error(error)
62	  }, [error])
63	
64	  return (
65	    <div>
66	      <h2>Something went wrong!</h2>
67	      <button
68	        onClick={
69	          // Attempt to recover by re-fetching and re-rendering the segment
70	          () => unstable_retry()
71	        }
72	      >
73	        Try again
74	      </button>
75	    </div>
76	  )
77	}
78	```
79	
80	`error.js` wraps a route segment and its nested children in a [React Error Boundary](https://react.dev/reference/react/Component#catching-rendering-errors-with-an-error-boundary). When an error throws within the boundary, the `error` component shows as the fallback UI.
81	
82	<Image
83	  alt="How error.js works"
84	  srcLight="/docs/light/error-overview.png"
85	  srcDark="/docs/dark/error-overview.png"
86	  width="1600"
87	  height="903"
88	/>
89	
90	> **Good to know**:
91	>
92	> - The [React DevTools](https://react.dev/learn/react-developer-tools) allow you to toggle error boundaries to test error states.
93	> - If you want errors to bubble up to the parent error boundary, you can `throw` when rendering the `error` component.
94	> - For component-level error recovery that aren't tied to route segments like [`error.js`](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/error), use the [`unstable_catchError`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/catchError) function.
95	
96	In the [component hierarchy](/docs/app/getting-started/project-structure#component-hierarchy), `error.js` wraps `loading.js`, `not-found.js`, `page.js`, and nested `layout.js` files in a React error boundary. It does **not** wrap the `layout.js` or `template.js` above it in the same segment. To handle errors in the root layout, use [`global-error.js`](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/error#global-error).
97	
98	## Reference
99	
100	### Props
101	
102	#### `error`
103	
104	An instance of an [`Error`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Error) object forwarded to the `error.js` Client Component.
105	
106	> **Good to know:** During development, the `Error` object forwarded to the client will be serialized and include the `message` of the original error for easier debugging. However, **this behavior is different in production** to avoid leaking potentially sensitive details included in the error to the client.
107	
108	#### `error.message`
109	
110	- Errors forwarded from Client Components show the original `Error` message.
111	- Errors forwarded from Server Components show a generic message with an identifier. This is to prevent leaking sensitive details. You can use the identifier, under `errors.digest`, to match the corresponding server-side logs.
112	
113	#### `error.digest`
114	
115	An automatically generated hash of the error thrown. It can be used to match the corresponding error in server-side logs.
116	
117	#### `unstable_retry`
118	
119	The cause of an error can sometimes be temporary. In these cases, trying again might resolve the issue.
120	
121	An error component can use the `unstable_retry()` function to prompt the user to attempt to recover from the error. When executed, the function will try to re-fetch and re-render the error boundary's children. If successful, the fallback error component is replaced with the result of the re-render.
122	
123	```tsx filename="app/dashboard/error.tsx" switcher
124	'use client' // Error boundaries must be Client Components
125	
126	export default function Error({
127	  error,
128	  unstable_retry,
129	}: {
130	  error: Error & { digest?: string }
131	  unstable_retry: () => void
132	}) {
133	  return (
134	    <div>
135	      <h2>Something went wrong!</h2>
136	      <button onClick={() => unstable_retry()}>Try again</button>
137	    </div>
138	  )
139	}
140	```
141	
142	```jsx filename="app/dashboard/error.js" switcher
143	'use client' // Error boundaries must be Client Components
144	
145	export default function Error({ error, unstable_retry }) {
146	  return (
147	    <div>
148	      <h2>Something went wrong!</h2>
149	      <button onClick={() => unstable_retry()}>Try again</button>
150	    </div>
151	  )
152	}
153	```
154	
155	#### `reset`
156	
157	In most cases, you should use [`unstable_retry()`](#unstable_retry) instead. However, if you have a specific reason to clear the error state and re-render the error boundary's children without re-fetching the contents, you can use the `reset()` function.
158	
159	## Examples
160	
161	### Global Error
162	
163	While less common, you can handle errors in the root layout or template using `global-error.jsx`, located in the root app directory, even when leveraging [internationalization](/docs/app/guides/internationalization). Global error UI must define its own `<html>` and `<body>` tags, global styles, fonts, or other dependencies that your error page requires. This file replaces the root layout or template when active.
164	
165	> **Good to know**: Error boundaries must be [Client Components](/docs/app/getting-started/server-and-client-components#using-client-components), which means that [`metadata` and `generateMetadata`](/docs/app/getting-started/metadata-and-og-images) exports are not supported in `global-error.jsx`. As an alternative, you can use the React [`<title>`](https://react.dev/reference/react-dom/components/title) component.
166	
167	```tsx filename="app/global-error.tsx" switcher
168	'use client' // Error boundaries must be Client Components
169	
170	export default function GlobalError({
171	  error,
172	  unstable_retry,
173	}: {
174	  error: Error & { digest?: string }
175	  unstable_retry: () => void
176	}) {
177	  return (
178	    // global-error must include html and body tags
179	    <html>
180	      <body>
181	        <h2>Something went wrong!</h2>
182	        <button onClick={() => unstable_retry()}>Try again</button>
183	      </body>
184	    </html>
185	  )
186	}
187	```
188	
189	```jsx filename="app/global-error.js" switcher
190	'use client' // Error boundaries must be Client Components
191	
192	export default function GlobalError({ error, unstable_retry }) {
193	  return (
194	    // global-error must include html and body tags
195	    <html>
196	      <body>
197	        <h2>Something went wrong!</h2>
198	        <button onClick={() => unstable_retry()}>Try again</button>
199	      </body>
200	    </html>
201	  )
202	}
203	```
204	
205	### Graceful error recovery with a custom error boundary
206	
207	When rendering fails on the client, it can be useful to show the last known server rendered UI for a better user experience.
208	
209	The `GracefullyDegradingErrorBoundary` is an example of a custom error boundary that captures and preserves the current HTML before an error occurs. If a rendering error happens, it re-renders the captured HTML and displays a persistent notification bar to inform the user.
210	
211	```tsx filename="app/dashboard/error.tsx" switcher
212	'use client'
213	
214	import React, { Component, ErrorInfo, ReactNode } from 'react'
215	
216	interface ErrorBoundaryProps {
217	  children: ReactNode
218	  onError?: (error: Error, errorInfo: ErrorInfo) => void
219	}
220	
221	interface ErrorBoundaryState {
222	  hasError: boolean
223	}
224	
225	export class GracefullyDegradingErrorBoundary extends Component<
226	  ErrorBoundaryProps,
227	  ErrorBoundaryState
228	> {
229	  private contentRef: React.RefObject<HTMLDivElement | null>
230	
231	  constructor(props: ErrorBoundaryProps) {
232	    super(props)
233	    this.state = { hasError: false }
234	    this.contentRef = React.createRef()
235	  }
236	
237	  static getDerivedStateFromError(_: Error): ErrorBoundaryState {
238	    return { hasError: true }
239	  }
240	
241	  componentDidCatch(error: Error, errorInfo: ErrorInfo) {
242	    if (this.props.onError) {
243	      this.props.onError(error, errorInfo)
244	    }
245	  }
246	
247	  render() {
248	    if (this.state.hasError) {
249	      // Render the current HTML content without hydration
250	      return (
251	        <>
252	          <div
253	            ref={this.contentRef}
254	            suppressHydrationWarning
255	            dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{
256	              __html: this.contentRef.current?.innerHTML || '',
257	            }}
258	          />
259	          <div className="fixed bottom-0 left-0 right-0 bg-red-600 text-white py-4 px-6 text-center">
260	            <p className="font-semibold">
261	              An error occurred during page rendering
262	            </p>
263	          </div>
264	        </>
265	      )
266	    }
267	
268	    return <div ref={this.contentRef}>{this.props.children}</div>
269	  }
270	}
271	
272	export default GracefullyDegradingErrorBoundary
273	```
274	
275	```jsx filename="app/dashboard/error.js" switcher
276	'use client'
277	
278	import React, { Component, createRef } from 'react'
279	
280	class GracefullyDegradingErrorBoundary extends Component {
281	  constructor(props) {
282	    super(props)
283	    this.state = { hasError: false }
284	    this.contentRef = createRef()
285	  }
286	
287	  static getDerivedStateFromError(_) {
288	    return { hasError: true }
289	  }
290	
291	  componentDidCatch(error, errorInfo) {
292	    if (this.props.onError) {
293	      this.props.onError(error, errorInfo)
294	    }
295	  }
296	
297	  render() {
298	    if (this.state.hasError) {
299	      // Render the current HTML content without hydration
300	      return (
301	        <>
302	          <div
303	            ref={this.contentRef}
304	            suppressHydrationWarning
305	            dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{
306	              __html: this.contentRef.current?.innerHTML || '',
307	            }}
308	          />
309	          <div className="fixed bottom-0 left-0 right-0 bg-red-600 text-white py-4 px-6 text-center">
310	            <p className="font-semibold">
311	              An error occurred during page rendering
312	            </p>
313	          </div>
314	        </>
315	      )
316	    }
317	
318	    return <div ref={this.contentRef}>{this.props.children}</div>
319	  }
320	}
321	
322	export default GracefullyDegradingErrorBoundary
323	```
324	
325	## Version History
326	
327	| Version   | Changes                                     |
328	| --------- | ------------------------------------------- |
329	| `v16.2.0` | `unstable_retry` prop added.                |
330	| `v15.2.0` | Also display `global-error` in development. |
331	| `v13.1.0` | `global-error` introduced.                  |
332	| `v13.0.0` | `error` introduced.                         |
333
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/loading.md`

```text
1	---
2	title: loading.js
3	description: API reference for the loading.js file.
4	---
5	
6	{/* AI agent hint: loading.js provides fallback UI but does not guarantee instant client-side navigations. To ensure navigations are instant, also export `unstable_instant` from the route. See docs/01-app/02-guides/instant-navigation.mdx */}
7	
8	The special file `loading.js` helps you create meaningful Loading UI with [React Suspense](https://react.dev/reference/react/Suspense). With this convention, you can show an [instant loading state](#instant-loading-states) from the server while the content of a route segment streams in. The new content is automatically swapped in once complete.
9	
10	<Image
11	  alt="Loading UI"
12	  srcLight="/docs/light/loading-ui.png"
13	  srcDark="/docs/dark/loading-ui.png"
14	  width="1600"
15	  height="691"
16	/>
17	
18	```tsx filename="app/feed/loading.tsx" switcher
19	export default function Loading() {
20	  // Or a custom loading skeleton component
21	  return <p>Loading...</p>
22	}
23	```
24	
25	```jsx filename="app/feed/loading.js" switcher
26	export default function Loading() {
27	  // Or a custom loading skeleton component
28	  return <p>Loading...</p>
29	}
30	```
31	
32	Inside the `loading.js` file, you can add any light-weight loading UI. You may find it helpful to use the [React Developer Tools](https://react.dev/learn/react-developer-tools) to manually toggle Suspense boundaries.
33	
34	By default, this file is a [Server Component](/docs/app/getting-started/server-and-client-components) - but can also be used as a Client Component through the `"use client"` directive.
35	
36	## Reference
37	
38	### Parameters
39	
40	Loading UI components do not accept any parameters.
41	
42	## Behavior
43	
44	### Navigation
45	
46	- The Fallback UI is [prefetched](/docs/app/getting-started/linking-and-navigating#prefetching), making navigation immediate unless prefetching hasn't completed.
47	- Navigation is interruptible, meaning changing routes does not need to wait for the content of the route to fully load before navigating to another route.
48	- Shared layouts remain interactive while new route segments load.
49	
50	### Instant Loading States
51	
52	An instant loading state is fallback UI that is shown immediately upon navigation. You can prerender loading indicators such as skeletons and spinners, or a small but meaningful part of future screens such as a cover photo, title, etc. This helps users understand the app is responding and provides a better user experience.
53	
54	Create a loading state by adding a `loading.js` file inside a folder.
55	
56	<Image
57	  alt="loading.js special file"
58	  srcLight="/docs/light/loading-special-file.png"
59	  srcDark="/docs/dark/loading-special-file.png"
60	  width="1600"
61	  height="606"
62	/>
63	
64	```tsx filename="app/dashboard/loading.tsx" switcher
65	export default function Loading() {
66	  // You can add any UI inside Loading, including a Skeleton.
67	  return <LoadingSkeleton />
68	}
69	```
70	
71	```jsx filename="app/dashboard/loading.js" switcher
72	export default function Loading() {
73	  // You can add any UI inside Loading, including a Skeleton.
74	  return <LoadingSkeleton />
75	}
76	```
77	
78	In the same folder, `loading.js` will be nested inside `layout.js`. It will automatically wrap the `page.js` file and any children below in a `<Suspense>` boundary.
79	
80	<Image
81	  alt="loading.js overview"
82	  srcLight="/docs/light/loading-overview.png"
83	  srcDark="/docs/dark/loading-overview.png"
84	  width="1600"
85	  height="768"
86	/>
87	
88	In the [component hierarchy](/docs/app/getting-started/project-structure#component-hierarchy), `loading.js` wraps `not-found.js`, `page.js`, and nested `layout.js` files in a `<Suspense>` boundary. It does **not** wrap the `layout.js`, `template.js`, or `error.js` in the same segment.
89	
90	> **Good to know**: If the layout accesses uncached or runtime data (e.g. `cookies()`, `headers()`, or uncached fetches), `loading.js` will not show a fallback for it.
91	>
92	> - **Without [Cache Components](/docs/app/getting-started/caching):** Navigation blocks until the layout finishes rendering.
93	> - **With [Cache Components](/docs/app/getting-started/caching):** Uncached or runtime data access in the layout must be explicitly wrapped in `<Suspense>`, otherwise Next.js guides you with a build-time error. The static shell streams first, and the uncached content fills in.
94	>
95	> To ensure instant navigation, move uncached data fetching from `layout.js` into `page.js`, or wrap the runtime data access in your layout in its own `<Suspense>` boundary. See [layout.js Caveats](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/layout#interaction-with-loadingjs) for details and examples.
96	
97	### SEO
98	
99	- For bots that only scrape static HTML, and cannot execute JavaScript like a full browser, such as Twitterbot, Next.js resolves [`generateMetadata`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/generate-metadata) before streaming UI, and metadata is placed in the `<head>` of the initial HTML.
100	- Otherwise, [streaming metadata](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/generate-metadata#streaming-metadata) may be used. Next.js automatically detects user agents to choose between blocking and streaming behavior.
101	- Since streaming is server-rendered, it does not impact SEO. You can use the [Rich Results Test](https://search.google.com/test/rich-results) tool from Google to see how your page appears to Google's web crawlers and view the serialized HTML ([source](https://web.dev/rendering-on-the-web/#seo-considerations)).
102	
103	### Status Codes
104	
105	When streaming, a `200` status code will be returned to signal that the request was successful.
106	
107	The server can still communicate errors or issues to the client within the streamed content itself, for example, when using [`redirect`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/redirect) or [`notFound`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/not-found). Because the response headers have already been sent to the client, the status code of the response cannot be updated.
108	
109	For example, when a 404 page is streamed to the client, Next.js includes a `<meta name="robots" content="noindex">` tag in the streamed HTML. This prevents search engines from indexing that URL even if the HTTP status is 200. See Google’s guidance on the [`robots` meta tag](https://developers.google.com/search/docs/crawling-indexing/robots-meta-tag).
110	
111	Some crawlers may label these responses as “soft 404s”. In the streaming case, this does not lead to indexation because the page is explicitly marked `noindex` in the HTML.
112	
113	If you need a 404 status, for compliance or analytics, ensure the resource exists before the response body is streamed, so that the server can set the HTTP status code.
114	
115	You can run this check in [`proxy`](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/proxy) to rewrite missing slugs to a not-found route, or [produce a 404 response](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/proxy#producing-a-response). Keep proxy checks fast, and avoid fetching full content there.
116	
117	<details>
118	<summary>When is the response body streamed?</summary>
119	
120	The response body starts streaming when a Suspense fallback renders (for example, a `loading.tsx`) or when a Server Component suspends under a `Suspense` boundary. Place `notFound()` before those boundaries and before any `await` that may suspend.
121	
122	To start streaming, the response headers must be set. This is why it is not possible to change the status code after streaming started.
123	
124	</details>
125	
126	### Browser limits
127	
128	[Some browsers](https://bugs.webkit.org/show_bug.cgi?id=252413) buffer a streaming response. You may not see the streamed response until the response exceeds 1024 bytes. This typically only affects “hello world” applications, but not real applications.
129	
130	## Platform Support
131	
132	| Deployment Option                                                   | Supported         |
133	| ------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------- |
134	| [Node.js server](/docs/app/getting-started/deploying#nodejs-server) | Yes               |
135	| [Docker container](/docs/app/getting-started/deploying#docker)      | Yes               |
136	| [Static export](/docs/app/getting-started/deploying#static-export)  | No                |
137	| [Adapters](/docs/app/getting-started/deploying#adapters)            | Platform-specific |
138	
139	Learn how to [configure streaming](/docs/app/guides/self-hosting#streaming-and-suspense) when self-hosting Next.js.
140	
141	## Examples
142	
143	### Streaming with Suspense
144	
145	In addition to `loading.js`, you can also manually create Suspense Boundaries for your own UI components. The App Router supports streaming with [Suspense](https://react.dev/reference/react/Suspense). See the [Streaming guide](/docs/app/guides/streaming) for more on how streaming works, including granular Suspense patterns, Route Handler streaming, and infrastructure considerations.
146	
147	`<Suspense>` works by wrapping a component that performs an asynchronous action (e.g. fetch data), showing fallback UI (e.g. skeleton, spinner) while it's happening, and then swapping in your component once the action completes.
148	
149	```tsx filename="app/dashboard/page.tsx" switcher
150	import { Suspense } from 'react'
151	import { PostFeed, Weather } from './Components'
152	
153	export default function Posts() {
154	  return (
155	    <section>
156	      <Suspense fallback={<p>Loading feed...</p>}>
157	        <PostFeed />
158	      </Suspense>
159	      <Suspense fallback={<p>Loading weather...</p>}>
160	        <Weather />
161	      </Suspense>
162	    </section>
163	  )
164	}
165	```
166	
167	```jsx filename="app/dashboard/page.js" switcher
168	import { Suspense } from 'react'
169	import { PostFeed, Weather } from './Components'
170	
171	export default function Posts() {
172	  return (
173	    <section>
174	      <Suspense fallback={<p>Loading feed...</p>}>
175	        <PostFeed />
176	      </Suspense>
177	      <Suspense fallback={<p>Loading weather...</p>}>
178	        <Weather />
179	      </Suspense>
180	    </section>
181	  )
182	}
183	```
184	
185	By using Suspense, you get the benefits of:
186	
187	1. **Streaming Server Rendering** - Progressively rendering HTML from the server to the client.
188	2. **Selective Hydration** - React prioritizes what components to make interactive first based on user interaction.
189	
190	For more Suspense examples and use cases, please see the [React Documentation](https://react.dev/reference/react/Suspense).
191	
192	## Version History
193	
194	| Version   | Changes               |
195	| --------- | --------------------- |
196	| `v13.0.0` | `loading` introduced. |
197
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/(client)/kalendar/page.tsx`

```text
1	import type { Metadata } from 'next';
2	import { Section, SectionTitle } from '@/components/ui';
3	import { Kalendar } from '@/components/client/kalendar';
4	
5	export const metadata: Metadata = {
6	  title: 'Kalendar',
7	};
8	
9	export default function KalendarPage() {
10	  return (

You are working on the MawarBiru project at /home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru. Complete two small tasks:

## Task 1: Fix middleware for API admin routes (5.1)

The current middleware at `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/middleware.ts` redirects unauthenticated `/admin/*` requests to `/admin/login`. But it needs to also handle `/api/admin/*` routes by returning a JSON 401 response instead of redirecting.

Current middleware:
```typescript
import { auth } from "@/lib/auth";
import { NextResponse } from "next/server";

export default auth((req) => {
  const { pathname } = req.nextUrl;
  if (pathname === "/admin/login") {
    return NextResponse.next();
  }
  if (!req.auth) {
    const loginUrl = new URL("/admin/login", req.nextUrl.origin);
    loginUrl.searchParams.set("callbackUrl", pathname);
    return NextResponse.redirect(loginUrl);
  }
  return NextResponse.next();
});

export const config = {
  matcher: ["/admin/:path*"],
};
```

Requirements:
- API routes under `/api/admin/*` should return `{ error: "Tidak dibenarkan" }` with 401 status for unauthenticated requests
- The matcher must also match `/api/admin/:path*`
- Keep middleware lightweight; no DB calls, JWT check only
- Login page still excluded from guard

## Task 2: Fix phone validation regex (5.6)

The file `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/lib/validations/temujanji.ts` has a phone regex that doesn't support the `+60` prefix. 

Current regex: `/^01[0-9]-?\d{7,8}$/`
Required regex per spec: `/^(\+?60)?0?1[0-9]-?[0-9]{7,8}$/`

This should accept: `0121234567`, `012-1234567`, `+60121234567`, `60121234567`

Edit both files. Do NOT create any new files.

You are working on the MawarBiru project at /home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru. Create error boundary files for the client-facing pages.

IMPORTANT: This is Next.js 16. Read the docs at `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/error.md` first — Next.js 16 uses `unstable_retry` instead of `reset` as the retry prop.

## Task: Create error.tsx and global-error.tsx

### 1. Create `app/(client)/error.tsx`
A branded error boundary for client pages. Requirements:
- `'use client'` directive (required for error boundaries)
- Props: `error: Error & { digest?: string }`, `unstable_retry: () => void`
- Uses MawarBiru luxury aesthetic: navy (#07203F) and cream (#EBDED4) palette
- Displays apologetic message in Bahasa Malaysia
- "Kembali ke Laman Utama" CTA button linking to `/`
- "Cuba Semula" button calling `unstable_retry()`
- Log error to console via `useEffect`
- Responsive: mobile centered stacked, desktop centered card with max-width
- Accessibility: focus moves to heading, CTA keyboard accessible
- Uses Tailwind classes matching the project's design system (font-serif for headings, font-sans for body)

### 2. Create `app/global-error.tsx`
For root layout failures. Requirements:
- `'use client'` directive
- MUST include its own `<html>` and `<body>` tags (replaces root layout when active)
- Same branded aesthetic as error.tsx
- Props: `error: Error & { digest?: string }`, `unstable_retry: () => void`
- "Cuba Semula" retry button
- Self-contained styling (inline Tailwind classes, no external deps)

Design guidelines:
- Background: bg-cream (#EBDED4)
- Text: text-navy (#07203F)  
- Heading font: font-serif (Playfair Display)
- Body font: font-sans (Inter)
- Button: navy bg, cream text, rounded-lg
- Keep it simple and self-contained (no data fetching)

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-20 09:46:52Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/error.md`

```text
1	---
2	title: error.js
3	description: API reference for the error.js special file.
4	related:
5	  title: Learn more about error handling
6	  links:
7	    - app/getting-started/error-handling
8	---
9	
10	An **error** file allows you to handle unexpected runtime errors and display fallback UI.
11	
12	<Image
13	  alt="error.js special file"
14	  srcLight="/docs/light/error-special-file.png"
15	  srcDark="/docs/dark/error-special-file.png"
16	  width="1600"
17	  height="606"
18	/>
19	
20	```tsx filename="app/dashboard/error.tsx" switcher
21	'use client' // Error boundaries must be Client Components
22	
23	import { useEffect } from 'react'
24	
25	export default function Error({
26	  error,
27	  unstable_retry,
28	}: {
29	  error: Error & { digest?: string }
30	  unstable_retry: () => void
31	}) {
32	  useEffect(() => {
33	    // Log the error to an error reporting service
34	    console.error(error)
35	  }, [error])
36	
37	  return (
38	    <div>
39	      <h2>Something went wrong!</h2>
40	      <button
41	        onClick={
42	          // Attempt to recover by re-fetching and re-rendering the segment
43	          () => unstable_retry()
44	        }
45	      >
46	        Try again
47	      </button>
48	    </div>
49	  )
50	}
51	```
52	
53	```jsx filename="app/dashboard/error.js" switcher
54	'use client' // Error boundaries must be Client Components
55	
56	import { useEffect } from 'react'
57	
58	export default function Error({ error, unstable_retry }) {
59	  useEffect(() => {
60	    // Log the error to an error reporting service
61	    console.error(error)
62	  }, [error])
63	
64	  return (
65	    <div>
66	      <h2>Something went wrong!</h2>
67	      <button
68	        onClick={
69	          // Attempt to recover by re-fetching and re-rendering the segment
70	          () => unstable_retry()
71	        }
72	      >
73	        Try again
74	      </button>
75	    </div>
76	  )
77	}
78	```
79	
80	`error.js` wraps a route segment and its nested children in a [React Error Boundary](https://react.dev/reference/react/Component#catching-rendering-errors-with-an-error-boundary). When an error throws within the boundary, the `error` component shows as the fallback UI.
81	
82	<Image
83	  alt="How error.js works"
84	  srcLight="/docs/light/error-overview.png"
85	  srcDark="/docs/dark/error-overview.png"
86	  width="1600"
87	  height="903"
88	/>
89	
90	> **Good to know**:
91	>
92	> - The [React DevTools](https://react.dev/learn/react-developer-tools) allow you to toggle error boundaries to test error states.
93	> - If you want errors to bubble up to the parent error boundary, you can `throw` when rendering the `error` component.
94	> - For component-level error recovery that aren't tied to route segments like [`error.js`](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/error), use the [`unstable_catchError`](/docs/app/api-reference/functions/catchError) function.
95	
96	In the [component hierarchy](/docs/app/getting-started/project-structure#component-hierarchy), `error.js` wraps `loading.js`, `not-found.js`, `page.js`, and nested `layout.js` files in a React error boundary. It does **not** wrap the `layout.js` or `template.js` above it in the same segment. To handle errors in the root layout, use [`global-error.js`](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/error#global-error).
97	
98	## Reference
99	
100	### Props
101	
102	#### `error`
103	
104	An instance of an [`Error`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Error) object forwarded to the `error.js` Client Component.
105	
106	> **Good to know:** During development, the `Error` object forwarded to the client will be serialized and include the `message` of the original error for easier debugging. However, **this behavior is different in production** to avoid leaking potentially sensitive details included in the error to the client.
107	
108	#### `error.message`
109	
110	- Errors forwarded from Client Components show the original `Error` message.
111	- Errors forwarded from Server Components show a generic message with an identifier. This is to prevent leaking sensitive details. You can use the identifier, under `errors.digest`, to match the corresponding server-side logs.
112	
113	#### `error.digest`
114	
115	An automatically generated hash of the error thrown. It can be used to match the corresponding error in server-side logs.
116	
117	#### `unstable_retry`
118	
119	The cause of an error can sometimes be temporary. In these cases, trying again might resolve the issue.
120	
121	An error component can use the `unstable_retry()` function to prompt the user to attempt to recover from the error. When executed, the function will try to re-fetch and re-render the error boundary's children. If successful, the fallback error component is replaced with the result of the re-render.
122	
123	```tsx filename="app/dashboard/error.tsx" switcher
124	'use client' // Error boundaries must be Client Components
125	
126	export default function Error({
127	  error,
128	  unstable_retry,
129	}: {
130	  error: Error & { digest?: string }
131	  unstable_retry: () => void
132	}) {
133	  return (
134	    <div>
135	      <h2>Something went wrong!</h2>
136	      <button onClick={() => unstable_retry()}>Try again</button>
137	    </div>
138	  )
139	}
140	```
141	
142	```jsx filename="app/dashboard/error.js" switcher
143	'use client' // Error boundaries must be Client Components
144	
145	export default function Error({ error, unstable_retry }) {
146	  return (
147	    <div>
148	      <h2>Something went wrong!</h2>
149	      <button onClick={() => unstable_retry()}>Try again</button>
150	    </div>
151	  )
152	}
153	```
154	
155	#### `reset`
156	
157	In most cases, you should use [`unstable_retry()`](#unstable_retry) instead. However, if you have a specific reason to clear the error state and re-render the error boundary's children without re-fetching the contents, you can use the `reset()` function.
158	
159	## Examples
160	
161	### Global Error
162	
163	While less common, you can handle errors in the root layout or template using `global-error.jsx`, located in the root app directory, even when leveraging [internationalization](/docs/app/guides/internationalization). Global error UI must define its own `<html>` and `<body>` tags, global styles, fonts, or other dependencies that your error page requires. This file replaces the root layout or template when active.
164	
165	> **Good to know**: Error boundaries must be [Client Components](/docs/app/getting-started/server-and-client-components#using-client-components), which means that [`metadata` and `generateMetadata`](/docs/app/getting-started/metadata-and-og-images) exports are not supported in `global-error.jsx`. As an alternative, you can use the React [`<title>`](https://react.dev/reference/react-dom/components/title) component.
166	
167	```tsx filename="app/global-error.tsx" switcher
168	'use client' // Error boundaries must be Client Components
169	
170	export default function GlobalError({
171	  error,
172	  unstable_retry,
173	}: {
174	  error: Error & { digest?: string }
175	  unstable_retry: () => void
176	}) {
177	  return (
178	    // global-error must include html and body tags
179	    <html>
180	      <body>
181	        <h2>Something went wrong!</h2>
182	        <button onClick={() => unstable_retry()}>Try again</button>
183	      </body>
184	    </html>
185	  )
186	}
187	```
188	
189	```jsx filename="app/global-error.js" switcher
190	'use client' // Error boundaries must be Client Components
191	
192	export default function GlobalError({ error, unstable_retry }) {
193	  return (
194	    // global-error must include html and body tags
195	    <html>
196	      <body>
197	        <h2>Something went wrong!</h2>
198	        <button onClick={() => unstable_retry()}>Try again</button>
199	      </body>
200	    </html>
201	  )
202	}
203	```
204	
205	### Graceful error recovery with a custom error boundary
206	
207	When rendering fails on the client, it can be useful to show the last known server rendered UI for a better user experience.
208	
209	The `GracefullyDegradingErrorBoundary` is an example of a custom error boundary that captures and preserves the current HTML before an error occurs. If a rendering error happens, it re-renders the captured HTML and displays a persistent notification bar to inform the user.
210	
211	```tsx filename="app/dashboard/error.tsx" switcher
212	'use client'
213	
214	import React, { Component, ErrorInfo, ReactNode } from 'react'
215	
216	interface ErrorBoundaryProps {
217	  children: ReactNode
218	  onError?: (error: Error, errorInfo: ErrorInfo) => void
219	}
220	
221	interface ErrorBoundaryState {
222	  hasError: boolean
223	}
224	
225	export class GracefullyDegradingErrorBoundary extends Component<
226	  ErrorBoundaryProps,
227	  ErrorBoundaryState
228	> {
229	  private contentRef: React.RefObject<HTMLDivElement | null>
230	
231	  constructor(props: ErrorBoundaryProps) {
232	    super(props)
233	    this.state = { hasError: false }
234	    this.contentRef = React.createRef()
235	  }
236	
237	  static getDerivedStateFromError(_: Error): ErrorBoundaryState {
238	    return { hasError: true }
239	  }
240	
241	  componentDidCatch(error: Error, errorInfo: ErrorInfo) {
242	    if (this.props.onError) {
243	      this.props.onError(error, errorInfo)
244	    }
245	  }
246	
247	  render() {
248	    if (this.state.hasError) {
249	      // Render the current HTML content without hydration
250	      return (
251	        <>
252	          <div
253	            ref={this.contentRef}
254	            suppressHydrationWarning
255	            dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{
256	              __html: this.contentRef.current?.innerHTML || '',
257	            }}
258	          />
259	          <div className="fixed bottom-0 left-0 right-0 bg-red-600 text-white py-4 px-6 text-center">
260	            <p className="font-semibold">
261	              An error occurred during page rendering
262	            </p>
263	          </div>
264	        </>
265	      )
266	    }
267	
268	    return <div ref={this.contentRef}>{this.props.children}</div>
269	  }
270	}
271	
272	export default GracefullyDegradingErrorBoundary
273	```
274	
275	```jsx filename="app/dashboard/error.js" switcher
276	'use client'
277	
278	import React, { Component, createRef } from 'react'
279	
280	class GracefullyDegradingErrorBoundary extends Component {
281	  constructor(props) {
282	    super(props)
283	    this.state = { hasError: false }
284	    this.contentRef = createRef()
285	  }
286	
287	  static getDerivedStateFromError(_) {
288	    return { hasError: true }
289	  }
290	
291	  componentDidCatch(error, errorInfo) {
292	    if (this.props.onError) {
293	      this.props.onError(error, errorInfo)
294	    }
295	  }
296	
297	  render() {
298	    if (this.state.hasError) {
299	      // Render the current HTML content without hydration
300	      return (
301	        <>
302	          <div
303	            ref={this.contentRef}
304	            suppressHydrationWarning
305	            dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{
306	              __html: this.contentRef.current?.innerHTML || '',
307	            }}
308	          />
309	          <div className="fixed bottom-0 left-0 right-0 bg-red-600 text-white py-4 px-6 text-center">
310	            <p className="font-semibold">
311	              An error occurred during page rendering
312	            </p>
313	          </div>
314	        </>
315	      )
316	    }
317	
318	    return <div ref={this.contentRef}>{this.props.children}</div>
319	  }
320	}
321	
322	export default GracefullyDegradingErrorBoundary
323	```
324	
325	## Version History
326	
327	| Version   | Changes                                     |
328	| --------- | ------------------------------------------- |
329	| `v16.2.0` | `unstable_retry` prop added.                |
330	| `v15.2.0` | Also display `global-error` in development. |
331	| `v13.1.0` | `global-error` introduced.                  |
332	| `v13.0.0` | `error` introduced.                         |
333
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-20 09:46:58Z)**_

Both edits are done. Here is a summary:

**Task 1 -- Middleware for API admin routes** (`/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/middleware.ts`):
- Added a check for `pathname.startsWith("/api/admin")` inside the unauthenticated block. These requests now receive a JSON response `{ error: "Tidak dibenarkan" }` with HTTP 401 instead of a redirect.
- Added `"/api/admin/:path*"` to the `matcher` config so the middleware runs on API admin routes.
- The login page exclusion and redirect behavior for non-API admin routes remain unchanged.

**Task 2 -- Phone validation regex** (`/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/lib/validations/temujanji.ts`):
- Updated the regex from `/^01[0-9]-?\d{7,8}$/` to `/^(\+?60)?0?1[0-9]-?[0-9]{7,8}$/`.
- This now accepts numbers with optional `+60` or `60` prefix, as well as the existing `01x` format with or without a dash.

You are working on the MawarBiru project at /home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru. Create skeleton loader components and loading.tsx files.

## Context

The existing skeleton component at `components/ui/skeleton.tsx` is a basic primitive:
```typescript
const skeletonVariants = cva('animate-pulse bg-navy/10', {
  variants: { variant: { text: 'h-4 w-full rounded', circle: 'rounded-full', rect: 'rounded-card' } },
  defaultVariants: { variant: 'text' },
});
```

The brand colors are defined as CSS variables in globals.css:
- Cream: #EBDED4 (--color-cream)
- Dusty rose: #D9AA90 (--color-dusty-rose)

The existing UI components are exported from `components/ui/index.ts`. Read this file first to see what's already exported.

Also read the existing pages to understand structure:
- `app/(client)/temujanji/page.tsx` - Uses Section, SectionTitle, TemujanjiForm
- `app/(client)/kalendar/page.tsx` - Uses Section, SectionTitle, Kalendar

## Tasks

### 1. Create `components/ui/skeleton-text.tsx`
- Renders animated placeholder lines (configurable `lines` count, default 3)
- Each line has slightly different width for natural look (100%, 85%, 70% pattern)
- Uses existing Skeleton component internally
- Has `aria-busy="true"`, `role="status"`, `aria-label="Memuatkan..."`
- Respects `prefers-reduced-motion` via Tailwind's `motion-reduce:` prefix

### 2. Create `components/ui/skeleton-card.tsx`  
- Card-shaped placeholder matching slot card design
- Contains a rect skeleton for image area + text skeletons for content
- Rounded corners matching --radius-card
- Has `aria-busy="true"`, `role="status"`, `aria-label="Memuatkan..."`

### 3. Create `components/ui/skeleton-calendar.tsx`
- Calendar grid shaped placeholder for /kalendar
- 7-column grid header (day labels) + 5 rows of day cells
- Uses existing Skeleton component
- Has `aria-busy="true"`, `role="status"`, `aria-label="Memuatkan..."`

### 4. Create `app/(client)/temujanji/loading.tsx`
- Shows skeleton matching the temujanji page layout
- Section with SectionTitle skeleton + form skeleton (input fields)

### 5. Create `app/(client)/kalendar/loading.tsx`
- Shows skeleton matching the kalendar page layout
- Section with SectionTitle skeleton + calendar grid skeleton

### 6. Update `components/ui/index.ts`
- Add exports for SkeletonText, SkeletonCard, SkeletonCalendar

Write all files. Use the existing Skeleton primitive where possible. Keep consistent with the project's Tailwind classes and design system.

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./components/ui/skeleton.tsx`

```text
1	import { forwardRef, type HTMLAttributes } from 'react';
2	import { cva, type VariantProps } from 'class-variance-authority';
3	import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';
4	
5	const skeletonVariants = cva('animate-pulse bg-navy/10', {
6	  variants: {
7	    variant: {
8	      text: 'h-4 w-full rounded',
9	      circle: 'rounded-full',
10	      rect: 'rounded-card',
11	    },
12	  },
13	  defaultVariants: {
14	    variant: 'text',
15	  },
16	});
17	
18	type SkeletonProps = HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> &
19	  VariantProps<typeof skeletonVariants> & {
20	    'data-testid'?: string;
21	  };
22	
23	const Skeleton = forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, SkeletonProps>(
24	  ({ className, variant, ...props }, ref) => {
25	    return (
26	      <div
27	        ref={ref}
28	        aria-hidden="true"
29	        className={cn(skeletonVariants({ variant }), className)}
30	        {...props}
31	      />
32	    );
33	  },
34	);
35	
36	Skeleton.displayName = 'Skeleton';
37	
38	export { Skeleton, skeletonVariants };
39	export type { SkeletonProps };
40
```
</details></tool-use>

1	import { forwardRef, type HTMLAttributes } from 'react';
2	import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';
3	import { Heading, type HeadingLevel } from './heading';
4	import { Text } from './text';
5	
6	type SectionTitleProps = HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> & {
7	  title: string;
8	  subtitle?: string;
9	  level?: HeadingLevel;
10	  'data-testid'?: string;
11	};
12	
13	const SectionTitle = forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, SectionTitleProps>(
14	  (
15	    { title, subtitle, level = 2, className, children, ...props },
16	    ref,

20	        ref={ref}

22	        {...props}

24	        <Heading level={level}>{title}</Heading>
25	        {subtitle && (
26	          <Text variant="lead" className="mt-2 text-dark/70">
27	            {subtitle}
28	          </Text>
29	        )}
30	        <div
31	          className="mx-auto mt-4 h-[2px] w-12 bg-dusty-rose"
32	          aria-hidden="true"
33	        />
34	        {children}
35	      </div>
36	    );
37	  },
38	);

40	SectionTitle.displayName = 'SectionTitle';
41	
42	export { SectionTitle };
43	export type { SectionTitleProps };

```tsx
'use client'

import { useEffect, useRef } from 'react'
import Link from 'next/link'

interface ErrorProps {
  error: Error & { digest?: string }
  unstable_retry: () => void
}

export default function Error({ error, unstable_retry }: ErrorProps) {
  const headingRef = useRef<HTMLHeadingElement>(null)

  useEffect(() => {
    console.error(error)
  }, [error])

  useEffect(() => {
    headingRef.current?.focus()
  }, [])

  return (
    <main className="flex min-h-screen items-center justify-center bg-cream px-4 py-12">
      <div className="w-full max-w-md rounded-lg bg-white p-8 shadow-elevated text-center sm:p-10">
        <h1
          ref={headingRef}
          tabIndex={-1}
          className="font-serif text-2xl font-bold text-navy outline-none sm:text-3xl"
        >
          Maaf, sesuatu telah berlaku
        </h1>

        <p className="mt-4 font-sans text-base text-navy/80">
          Kami mohon maaf atas kesulitan ini. Sila cuba semula atau kembali ke
          laman utama.
        </p>

        {error.digest && (
          <p className="mt-2 font-sans text-xs text-navy/50">
            Rujukan ralat: {error.digest}
          </p>
        )}

        <div className="mt-8 flex flex-col gap-3 sm:flex-row sm:justify-center">
          <button
            type="button"
            onClick={() => unstable_retry()}
            className="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-lg bg-navy px-6 py-3 font-sans text-sm font-medium text-cream transition-colors hover:bg-navy-light focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-navy"
          >
            Cuba Semula
          </button>

          <Link
            href="/"
            className="inline-flex items-center justify-center rounded-lg border border-navy px-6 py-3 font-sans text-sm font-medium text-navy transition-colors hover:bg-navy-50 focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-offset-2 focus-visible:outline-navy"
          >
            Kembali ke Laman Utama
          </Link>
        </div>
      </div>
    </main>
  )
}

```

```

You are working on the MawarBiru project at /home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru. Enhance the EmptyState component.

## Current component at `components/ui/empty-state.tsx`:
```typescript
'use client';
import { type ReactNode } from 'react';
import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';

interface EmptyStateAction {
  label: string;
  onClick: () => void;
}

interface EmptyStateProps {
  title: string;
  description?: string;
  icon?: ReactNode;
  action?: EmptyStateAction;
  className?: string;
  'data-testid'?: string;
}

function EmptyState({ title, description, icon, action, className, ...props }: EmptyStateProps) {
  return (
    <div className={cn('flex flex-col items-center justify-center py-12 px-6 text-center', className)} data-testid={props['data-testid']}>
      {icon && <div className="mb-4 text-navy/30" aria-hidden="true">{icon}</div>}
      <h3 className="font-serif text-lg font-semibold text-navy/70 mb-1">{title}</h3>
      {description && <p className="text-sm text-navy/50 max-w-sm mb-6">{description}</p>}
      {action && (
        <button type="button" onClick={action.onClick} className={cn('inline-flex items-center justify-center', 'h-10 px-4 text-sm font-medium', 'rounded-button', 'bg-navy text-cream hover:bg-navy-light active:bg-navy-dark', 'transition-colors duration-150 motion-reduce:transition-none', 'focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2 focus-visible:ring-dusty-rose')}>
          {action.label}
        </button>
      )}
    </div>
  );
}

export { EmptyState };
export type { EmptyStateProps, EmptyStateAction };
```

## Required changes:

1. **Add `role="status"`** to the container div so screen readers announce the empty state
2. **Add optional WhatsApp CTA link** — new optional prop `whatsAppMessage?: string` that renders a secondary link to `https://wa.me/{NEXT_PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER}?text={encoded message}` below or beside the action button
   - The WhatsApp number comes from `process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER`
   - Render as an `<a>` tag with `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"`
   - Style it as a secondary/outline button variant
   - `aria-label="Hubungi kami melalui WhatsApp"`
3. **Update the types** to export the new prop

Keep the existing functionality intact. Only modify `components/ui/empty-state.tsx` using the Edit tool. Read the file first.

```tsx
import { type HTMLAttributes } from 'react';
import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';
import { Skeleton } from './skeleton';

const LINE_WIDTHS = ['w-full', 'w-[85%]', 'w-[70%]'] as const;

type SkeletonTextProps = HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> & {
  lines?: number;
  'data-testid'?: string;
};

function SkeletonText({
  lines = 3,
  className,
  ...props
}: SkeletonTextProps) {
  return (
    <div
      role="status"
      aria-busy="true"
      aria-label="Memuatkan..."
      className={cn('space-y-3', className)}
      {...props}
    >
      {Array.from({ length: lines }, (_, i) => (
        <Skeleton
          key={i}
          variant="text"
          className={cn(
            LINE_WIDTHS[i % LINE_WIDTHS.length],
            'motion-reduce:animate-none',
          )}
        />
      ))}
    </div>
  );
}

SkeletonText.displayName = 'SkeletonText';

export { SkeletonText };
export type { SkeletonTextProps };

```

'use client'

import { useEffect, useRef } from 'react'

interface GlobalErrorProps {
  error: Error & { digest?: string }
  unstable_retry: () => void
}

export default function GlobalError({
  error,
  unstable_retry,
}: GlobalErrorProps) {
  const headingRef = useRef<HTMLHeadingElement>(null)

  useEffect(() => {
    console.error(error)
  }, [error])

  useEffect(() => {
    headingRef.current?.focus()
  }, [])

  return (
    <html lang="ms">
      <body
        style={{
          margin: 0,
          fontFamily: 'Inter, system-ui, sans-serif',
          backgroundColor: '#EBDED4',
          color: '#07203F',
        }}
      >
        <main
          style={{
            display: 'flex',
            minHeight: '100vh',
            alignItems: 'center',
            justifyContent: 'center',
            padding: '3rem 1rem',
          }}
        >
          <div
            style={{
              width: '100%',
              maxWidth: '28rem',
              borderRadius: '0.75rem',
              backgroundColor: '#FFFFFF',
              padding: '2.5rem 2rem',
              textAlign: 'center',
              boxShadow:
                '0 10px 15px -3px rgba(2, 0, 13, 0.1), 0 4px 6px -2px rgba(2, 0, 13, 0.05)',
            }}
          >
            <h1
              ref={headingRef}
              tabIndex={-1}
              style={{
                fontFamily: '"Playfair Display", Georgia, serif',
                fontSize: '1.75rem',
                fontWeight: 700,
                color: '#07203F',
                margin: 0,
                outline: 'none',
              }}
            >
              Maaf, sesuatu telah berlaku
            </h1>

            <p
              style={{
                marginTop: '1rem',
                fontSize: '1rem',
                lineHeight: 1.6,
                color: 'rgba(7, 32, 63, 0.8)',
              }}
            >
              Kami mohon maaf atas kesulitan ini. Sila cuba semula.
            </p>

            {error.digest && (
              <p
                style={{
                  marginTop: '0.5rem',
                  fontSize: '0.75rem',
                  color: 'rgba(7, 32, 63, 0.5)',
                }}
              >
                Rujukan ralat: {error.digest}
              </p>
            )}

            <div style={{ marginTop: '2rem' }}>
              <button
                type="button"
                onClick={() => unstable_retry()}
                style={{
                  display: 'inline-flex',
                  alignItems: 'center',
                  justifyContent: 'center',
                  borderRadius: '0.5rem',
                  backgroundColor: '#07203F',
                  color: '#EBDED4',
                  border: 'none',
                  padding: '0.75rem 1.5rem',
                  fontSize: '0.875rem',
                  fontWeight: 500,
                  fontFamily: 'Inter, system-ui, sans-serif',
                  cursor: 'pointer',
                }}
              >
                Cuba Semula
              </button>
            </div>
          </div>
        </main>
      </body>
    </html>
  )
}

```

```tsx
import { type HTMLAttributes } from 'react';
import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';
import { Skeleton } from './skeleton';

const DAY_LABELS = ['Isn', 'Sel', 'Rab', 'Kha', 'Jum', 'Sab', 'Ahd'] as const;
const ROWS = 5;

type SkeletonCalendarProps = HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement> & {
  'data-testid'?: string;
};

function SkeletonCalendar({
  className,
  ...props
}: SkeletonCalendarProps) {
  return (
    <div
      role="status"
      aria-busy="true"
      aria-label="Memuatkan..."
      className={cn('w-full', className)}
      {...props}
    >
      {/* Month header */}
      <div className="mb-4 flex items-center justify-between">
        <Skeleton
          variant="text"
          className="h-6 w-32 motion-reduce:animate-none"
        />
        <div className="flex gap-2">
          <Skeleton
            variant="rect"
            className="h-8 w-8 motion-reduce:animate-none"
          />
          <Skeleton
            variant="rect"
            className="h-8 w-8 motion-reduce:animate-none"
          />
        </div>
      </div>

      {/* Day labels header */}
      <div className="grid grid-cols-7 gap-1 mb-1">
        {DAY_LABELS.map((day) => (
          <div
            key={day}
            className="text-center text-xs text-dark/40 py-2"
            aria-hidden="true"
          >
            {day}
          </div>
        ))}
      </div>

      {/* Day cells grid */}
      <div className="grid grid-cols-7 gap-1">
        {Array.from({ length: ROWS * 7 }, (_, i) => (
          <Skeleton
            key={i}
            variant="rect"
            className="aspect-square w-full motion-reduce:animate-none"
          />
        ))}
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

SkeletonCalendar.displayName = 'SkeletonCalendar';

export { SkeletonCalendar };
export type { SkeletonCalendarProps };

```

```text
File created successfully at: /home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/components/ui/skeleton-calendar.tsx
```
</details></tool-use>
