import { Section } from '@/components/ui/section';
import { Skeleton } from '@/components/ui/skeleton';
import { SkeletonText } from '@/components/ui/skeleton-text';

export default function TemujanjiLoading() {
  return (
    <Section bg="cream" data-testid="temujanji-loading">
      {/* SectionTitle skeleton */}
      <div className="text-center">
        <Skeleton
          variant="text"
          className="mx-auto h-8 w-48 motion-reduce:animate-none"
        />
        <Skeleton
          variant="rect"
          className="mx-auto mt-4 h-[2px] w-12 motion-reduce:animate-none"
        />
      </div>

      {/* Form skeleton */}
      <div
        className="mx-auto mt-8 max-w-2xl space-y-6"
        role="status"
        aria-busy="true"
        aria-label="Memuatkan..."
      >
        {/* Input fields */}
        {Array.from({ length: 4 }, (_, i) => (
          <div key={i} className="space-y-2">
            <Skeleton
              variant="text"
              className="h-4 w-24 motion-reduce:animate-none"
            />
            <Skeleton
              variant="rect"
              className="h-10 w-full motion-reduce:animate-none"
            />
          </div>
        ))}

        {/* Textarea field */}
        <div className="space-y-2">
          <Skeleton
            variant="text"
            className="h-4 w-20 motion-reduce:animate-none"
          />
          <Skeleton
            variant="rect"
            className="h-24 w-full motion-reduce:animate-none"
          />
        </div>

        {/* Submit button */}
        <Skeleton
          variant="rect"
          className="h-10 w-full motion-reduce:animate-none"
        />
      </div>
    </Section>
  );
}

```

1	'use client';
2	
3	import { type ReactNode } from 'react';
4	import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';
5	
6	interface EmptyStateAction {
7	  label: string;
8	  onClick: () => void;
9	}
10	
11	interface EmptyStateProps {
12	  title: string;
13	  description?: string;
14	  icon?: ReactNode;
15	  action?: EmptyStateAction;
16	  whatsAppMessage?: string;
17	  className?: string;
18	  'data-testid'?: string;
19	}
20	
21	function EmptyState({
22	  title,
23	  description,
24	  icon,
25	  action,
26	  whatsAppMessage,
27	  className,
28	  ...props
29	}: EmptyStateProps) {
30	  const whatsAppNumber = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_WHATSAPP_NUMBER;
31	  const whatsAppHref =
32	    whatsAppNumber && whatsAppMessage
33	      ? `https://wa.me/${whatsAppNumber}?text=${encodeURIComponent(whatsAppMessage)}`
34	      : undefined;
35	
36	  return (
37	    <div
38	      role="status"
39	      className={cn(
40	        'flex flex-col items-center justify-center py-12 px-6 text-center',
41	        className,
42	      )}
43	      data-testid={props['data-testid']}
44	    >
45	      {icon && (
46	        <div className="mb-4 text-navy/30" aria-hidden="true">
47	          {icon}
48	        </div>
49	      )}
50	      <h3 className="font-serif text-lg font-semibold text-navy/70 mb-1">
51	        {title}
52	      </h3>
53	      {description && (
54	        <p className="text-sm text-navy/50 max-w-sm mb-6">{description}</p>
55	      )}
56	      {action && (
57	        <button
58	          type="button"
59	          onClick={action.onClick}
60	          className={cn(
61	            'inline-flex items-center justify-center',
62	            'h-10 px-4 text-sm font-medium',
63	            'rounded-button',
64	            'bg-navy text-cream hover:bg-navy-light active:bg-navy-dark',
65	            'transition-colors duration-150 motion-reduce:transition-none',
66	            'focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2 focus-visible:ring-dusty-rose',
67	          )}
68	        >
69	          {action.label}
70	        </button>
71	      )}
72	      {whatsAppHref && (
73	        <a
74	          href={whatsAppHref}
75	          target="_blank"
76	          rel="noopener noreferrer"
77	          aria-label="Hubungi kami melalui WhatsApp"
78	          className={cn(
79	            'inline-flex items-center justify-center',
80	            'h-10 px-4 text-sm font-medium',
81	            'rounded-button',
82	            'border border-navy/30 text-navy/70 bg-transparent',
83	            'hover:bg-navy/5 active:bg-navy/10',
84	            'transition-colors duration-150 motion-reduce:transition-none',
85	            'focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2 focus-visible:ring-dusty-rose',
86	            action ? 'mt-3' : '',
87	          )}
88	        >
89	          Hubungi via WhatsApp
90	        </a>
91	      )}
92	    </div>
93	  );
94	}
95	
96	export { EmptyState };
97	export type { EmptyStateProps, EmptyStateAction };
98

ƒ  (Dynamic)  server-rendered on demand
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	# Phase 6: Admin Surface
2	
3	All admin modules use STANDARD rigor. Functionality over finesse. Desktop-first, conventional CRUD patterns. One task per resource unless complexity warrants splitting.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## 6.1 Admin Layout Shell and Authentication
8	
9	**Description:** Build the admin layout wrapper: sidebar navigation, top bar with logout, and the NextAuth.js credentials login page. This is the frame for all admin modules.
10	
11	**Acceptance criteria:**
12	- [ ] `/admin/login` page with email + password form
13	- [ ] NextAuth.js credentials provider authenticates against single admin User record
14	- [ ] Password stored as bcrypt hash in DB
15	- [ ] Successful login redirects to `/admin` dashboard
16	- [ ] Failed login shows inline error: "E-mel atau kata laluan salah"
17	- [ ] Admin layout has collapsible sidebar with navigation links to all modules
18	- [ ] Sidebar nav items: Dashboard, Slot Temujanji, Temujanji, Slot Tempahan, Tempahan, Pakej, Pelanggan
19	- [ ] Top bar shows admin name/email and logout button
20	- [ ] Logout clears session and redirects to `/admin/login`
21	- [ ] Sidebar collapses to icons on narrow viewports (tablet)
22	
23	**Technical notes:**
24	- Use NextAuth.js `CredentialsProvider` with Prisma adapter for session persistence
25	- Admin layout in `app/admin/layout.tsx` — wraps all admin routes
26	- Login page at `app/admin/login/page.tsx` — excluded from admin layout (own layout)
27	- Seed script creates initial admin user with hashed password
28	- Sidebar: simple `<nav>` with links, active state highlighting
29	
30	**Dependencies:** 1.x (Foundation — NextAuth, Prisma, DB schema)
31	**Estimate:** M
32	
33	---
34	
35	## 6.2 Admin Dashboard
36	
37	**Description:** Simple overview page at `/admin` showing key counts and recent activity. Not a complex analytics dashboard — just quick-glance numbers.
38	
39	**Acceptance criteria:**
40	- [ ] Shows count cards: Temujanji hari ini, Tempahan bulan ini, Jumlah Pelanggan, Jumlah Pakej
41	- [ ] Shows 5 most recent temujanji bookings (nama, tarikh, status)
42	- [ ] Shows 5 most recent tempahan (nama, tarikh, pakej)
43	- [ ] Each card links to its respective management page
44	- [ ] Data fetched server-side (SSR or server component)
45	
46	**Technical notes:**
47	- Use server components for data fetching
48	- Simple Prisma aggregate queries (`count`, `findMany` with `take: 5`)
49	- Basic card grid layout — no charts or complex visualization needed
50	
51	**Dependencies:** 6.1
52	**Estimate:** S
53	
54	---
55	
56	## 6.3 Pengurusan Slot Temujanji (Consultation Slot Management)
57	
58	**Description:** Full CRUD for managing 2-hour consultation time slots. Admin creates available slots; visitors see and book them on the public temujanji page.
59	
60	**Acceptance criteria:**
61	- [ ] List view: table with columns Tarikh, Masa Mula, Masa Tamat, Status, Tindakan
62	- [ ] Table is searchable by date and filterable by status (Tersedia/Ditempah)
63	- [ ] Pagination on the list (default 20 per page)
64	- [ ] Create form: Tarikh (date picker), Masa Mula (time picker), Masa Tamat (auto-calculated as Mula + 2 hours, editable)
65	- [ ] Validation: Masa Tamat must be after Masa Mula, no overlapping slots on same date
66	- [ ] Edit existing slot (only if status is Tersedia)
67	- [ ] Delete slot (only if status is Tersedia; ditempah slots show warning before delete)
68	- [ ] Bulk create: option to generate multiple slots for a date (e.g., 10:00-12:00, 14:00-16:00, 16:00-18:00)
69	- [ ] Status shown as badge: Tersedia (green), Ditempah (amber)
70	
71	**Technical notes:**
72	- Use shared DataTable component with server-side pagination
73	- API routes: `GET/POST /api/admin/slot-temujanji`, `PUT/DELETE /api/admin/slot-temujanji/[id]`
74	- Masa Tamat auto-fills when Masa Mula changes (convenience, not enforced)
75	- Overlap check in API: query existing slots for same tarikh, check time range intersection
76	
77	**Dependencies:** 6.1, 1.x (Prisma schema — SlotTemujanji)
78	**Estimate:** M
79	
80	---
81	
82	## 6.4 Pengurusan Temujanji (Consultation Booking Management)
83	
84	**Description:** View and manage all consultation bookings made by visitors. Admin can see pelanggan details, slot info, and notes. Admin can update status or delete bookings.
85	
86	**Acceptance criteria:**
87	- [ ] List view: table with columns Pelanggan (Nama + No. Telefon), Tarikh, Masa, Nota (truncated), Status, Tindakan
88	- [ ] Table searchable by pelanggan name or phone number
89	- [ ] Filterable by date range and status
90	- [ ] Pagination (default 20 per page)
91	- [ ] Click row to see full detail view (pelanggan info, slot details, full nota)
92	- [ ] Admin can delete a temujanji (slot reverts to Tersedia)
93	- [ ] Admin can manually change slot status if needed
94	- [ ] No create action from this page — temujanji are created by visitors via public page
95	
96	**Technical notes:**
97	- API routes: `GET /api/admin/temujanji`, `DELETE /api/admin/temujanji/[id]`
98	- Include Pelanggan and SlotTemujanji relations in query
99	- Deleting temujanji should update the linked SlotTemujanji status back to TERSEDIA (transaction)
100	
101	**Dependencies:** 6.1, 6.3
102	**Estimate:** S
103	
104	---
105	
106	## 6.5 Pengurusan Slot Tempahan (Venue Booking Slot Management)
107	
108	**Description:** CRUD for venue booking date slots. Each slot represents a single date the venue is available for booking. Includes a calendar view for visual management.
109	
110	**Acceptance criteria:**
111	- [ ] Calendar view showing all dates with slots (colour-coded: Tersedia green, Ditempah amber/red)
112	- [ ] Click on empty date to create new slot for that date
113	- [ ] Click on existing slot to edit or delete
114	- [ ] Table/list view toggle as alternative to calendar
115	- [ ] Create form: Tarikh (date picker), Status defaults to Tersedia
116	- [ ] Bulk create: select date range to create multiple slots at once
117	- [ ] Delete only Tersedia slots; Ditempah slots require confirmation and cascade warning
118	- [ ] Month navigation on calendar view
119	
120	**Technical notes:**
121	- Reuse calendar grid component (shared with public Kalendar, but interactive version)
122	- API routes: `GET/POST /api/admin/slot-tempahan`, `PUT/DELETE /api/admin/slot-tempahan/[id]`
123	- Calendar rendering: server-fetch all slots for displayed month, render client-side grid
124	- Bulk create endpoint accepts `{ startDate, endDate }` and creates one slot per day
125	
126	**Dependencies:** 6.1, 1.x (Prisma schema — SlotTempahan)
127	**Estimate:** M
128	
129	---
130	
131	## 6.6 Pengurusan Tempahan (Venue Booking Management)
132	
133	**Description:** Manage venue bookings. Admin creates bookings by clicking available slot dates, then fills in booking details. Booked events appear on the public `/kalendar`.
134	
135	**Acceptance criteria:**
136	- [ ] Calendar view of Slot Tempahan; clicking a Tersedia slot opens booking creation form
137	- [ ] Creation form fields: Nama Tempahan (text), Pakej (select dropdown from existing Pakej), Pelanggan (searchable select from existing Pelanggan)
138	- [ ] No kategori field — Pakej acts as the category
139	- [ ] On submit: Tempahan created, SlotTempahan status set to DITEMPAH (transaction)
140	- [ ] List view: table with columns Nama Tempahan, Tarikh, Pakej, Pelanggan, Tindakan
141	- [ ] Table searchable by nama tempahan or pelanggan name
142	- [ ] Filterable by pakej and date range
143	- [ ] Edit booking: can change nama tempahan, pakej, pelanggan (but not slot date)
144	- [ ] Delete booking: slot reverts to Tersedia (transaction)
145	- [ ] Pagination on list view
146	- [ ] Calendar events colour-coded by Pakej.warna (matches public kalendar)
147	
148	**Technical notes:**
149	- API routes: `GET/POST /api/admin/tempahan`, `PUT/DELETE /api/admin/tempahan/[id]`
150	- Creation flow: calendar click -> captures slotTempahanId -> passes to form
151	- Pakej select: fetch all pakej with `id, namaPakej` for dropdown
152	- Pelanggan select: searchable/autocomplete dropdown, fetch with debounced search
153	- Transactions: creating/deleting tempahan must atomically update SlotTempahan status
154	
155	**Dependencies:** 6.1, 6.5, 6.7, 6.9 (needs Pakej and Pelanggan to exist)
156	**Estimate:** L
157	
158	---
159	
160	## 6.7 Pengurusan Pakej (Package Management)
161	
162	**Description:** Full CRUD for packages including dynamic tiers (pax + price combos), dynamic sections (title + bullet items), colour picker for calendar coding, and image upload.
163	
164	**Acceptance criteria:**
165	- [ ] List view: table with columns Nama Pakej, Warna (colour swatch), Bilangan Tier, Gambar (thumbnail), Tindakan
166	- [ ] Create/Edit form fields:
167	  - Nama Pakej (text, required)
168	  - Warna (colour picker input, hex value, required) — used for calendar colour-coding
169	  - Gambar (image upload, single file, optional)
170	- [ ] Tiers section: dynamic list, each row has Bilangan Pax (number) + Harga (number, RM formatted)
171	  - Add tier button, remove tier button per row
172	  - Minimum 1 tier required
173	- [ ] Sections section: dynamic list, each row has Tajuk (text) + Items (multi-line or tag input for bullet items)
174	  - Add section button, remove section button per row
175	  - Items stored as JSON array of strings
176	- [ ] Image upload: accept JPG/PNG, max 5MB, stored locally on VPS filesystem (or `/public/uploads/`)
177	- [ ] Delete pakej: only if no Tempahan references it (show warning if referenced)
178	- [ ] Colour picker shows preview swatch next to the input
179	
180	**Technical notes:**
181	- API routes: `GET/POST /api/admin/pakej`, `PUT/DELETE /api/admin/pakej/[id]`
182	- Image upload: use Next.js API route with `formidable` or `multer`, save to `public/uploads/pakej/`
183	- Dynamic tiers/sections: managed as nested arrays in form state, sent as JSON
184	- On save: upsert PakejTier and PakejSection records (delete removed, create new, update existing)
185	- Colour picker: HTML5 `<input type="color">` is sufficient, or a small library for better UX
186	- PakejSection items field: store as JSON array in the database column
187	
188	**Dependencies:** 6.1, 1.x (Prisma schema — Pakej, PakejTier, PakejSection)
189	**Estimate:** L
190	
191	---
192	
193	## 6.8 Pengurusan Pakej — Image Upload Utility
194	
195	**Description:** Shared image upload utility for the Pakej module. Handles file validation, storage, and serving. Kept as a separate concern for reusability.
196	
197	**Acceptance criteria:**
198	- [ ] Upload endpoint accepts multipart form data
199	- [ ] Validates file type (JPG, PNG only) and size (max 5MB)
200	- [ ] Generates unique filename (UUID + original extension)
201	- [ ] Saves to `public/uploads/pakej/` directory
202	- [ ] Returns the public URL path for the saved image
203	- [ ] Old image is deleted when a new one is uploaded (update case)
204	- [ ] Handles missing/corrupt file gracefully with error message
205	
206	**Technical notes:**
207	- API route: `POST /api/admin/upload`
208	- Use `fs.writeFile` for saving (VPS filesystem, not cloud storage)
209	- Ensure `public/uploads/` is in `.gitignore`
210	- Return path like `/uploads/pakej/<uuid>.jpg` (Next.js serves from `public/`)
211	
212	**Dependencies:** 6.1
213	**Estimate:** S
214	
215	---
216	
217	## 6.9 Pengurusan Pelanggan (Customer Management)
218	
219	**Description:** View and manage customer records. Customers are auto-created when visitors book temujanji, or manually created by admin.
220	
221	**Acceptance criteria:**
222	- [ ] List view: table with columns Nama, No. Telefon, Nota (truncated), Jumlah Temujanji, Jumlah Tempahan, Tindakan
223	- [ ] Table searchable by nama or phone number
224	- [ ] Pagination (default 20 per page)
225	- [ ] Create form: Nama (required), No. Telefon (required, Malaysian format), Nota (optional)
226	- [ ] Edit existing pelanggan
227	- [ ] Delete pelanggan: only if no linked Temujanji or Tempahan (show warning with counts if referenced)
228	- [ ] Click row to view detail: full info + list of linked temujanji and tempahan
229	
230	**Technical notes:**
231	- API routes: `GET/POST /api/admin/pelanggan`, `PUT/DELETE /api/admin/pelanggan/[id]`
232	- Include relation counts in list query: `_count: { temujanji: true, tempahan: true }`
233	- Phone validation: reuse the same Zod schema pattern from 5.6
234	- Deduplication: on create, check if phone number already exists and warn (don't block)
235	
236	**Dependencies:** 6.1, 1.x (Prisma schema — Pelanggan)
237	**Estimate:** M
238
```

Phase 6: **Admin Surface** — 9 tasks (6.1–6.9). Standard rigor, desktop-first CRUD. Here's the dependency order:

```
6.1 Admin Layout + Auth (foundation for all admin)
├── 6.2 Dashboard (S)
├── 6.3 Slot Temujanji CRUD (M)
│   └── 6.4 Temujanji Management (S)
├── 6.5 Slot Tempahan CRUD (M)
│   └── 6.6 Tempahan Management (L) ← also needs 6.7, 6.9
├── 6.7 Pakej CRUD (L)
│   └── 6.8 Image Upload Utility (S)
└── 6.9 Pelanggan CRUD (M)
```

**Recommended approach:**
1. **First:** 6.1 (Admin layout + login) — everything depends on it
2. **Parallel batch:** 6.2, 6.3, 6.5, 6.7, 6.8, 6.9 — all independent after 6.1
3. **Final batch:** 6.4 and 6.6 — depend on the above

Want me to start with 6.1, then fan out the rest in parallel?

 `./lib/auth.ts`

```text
1	import NextAuth from "next-auth";
2	import Credentials from "next-auth/providers/credentials";
3	import { compare } from "bcryptjs";
4	import { prisma } from "@/lib/db";
5	
6	declare module "next-auth" {
7	  interface Session {
8	    user: {
9	      id: string;
10	      nama: string;
11	      email: string;
12	    };
13	  }
14	
15	  interface User {
16	    nama: string;
17	  }
18	}
19	
20	declare module "@auth/core/jwt" {
21	  interface JWT {
22	    id: string;
23	    nama: string;
24	  }
25	}
26	
27	export const { handlers, auth, signIn, signOut } = NextAuth({
28	  secret: process.env.NEXTAUTH_SECRET,
29	  session: {
30	    strategy: "jwt",
31	    maxAge: 24 * 60 * 60, // 24 hours
32	  },
33	  pages: {
34	    signIn: "/admin/login",
35	  },
36	  providers: [
37	    Credentials({
38	      name: "credentials",
39	      credentials: {
40	        email: { label: "E-mel", type: "email" },
41	        password: { label: "Kata Laluan", type: "password" },
42	      },
43	      async authorize(credentials) {
44	        const email =
45	          typeof credentials?.email === "string" ? credentials.email : "";
46	        const password =
47	          typeof credentials?.password === "string" ? credentials.password : "";
48	
49	        if (!email || !password) {
50	          throw new Error("E-mel atau kata laluan tidak sah");
51	        }
52	
53	        const user = await prisma.user.findUnique({
54	          where: { email },
55	        });
56	
57	        if (!user) {
58	          throw new Error("E-mel atau kata laluan tidak sah");
59	        }
60	
61	        const isPasswordValid = await compare(password, user.passwordHash);
62	
63	        if (!isPasswordValid) {
64	          throw new Error("E-mel atau kata laluan tidak sah");
65	        }
66	
67	        return {
68	          id: user.id,
69	          email: user.email,
70	          nama: user.nama,
71	        };
72	      },
73	    }),
74	  ],
75	  callbacks: {
76	    jwt({ token, user }) {
77	      if (user) {
78	        token.id = user.id as string;
79	        token.nama = (user as { nama: string }).nama;
80	      }
81	      return token;
82	    },
83	    session({ session, token }) {
84	      return {
85	        ...session,
86	        user: {
87	          ...session.user,
88	          id: token.id,
89	          nama: token.nama,
90	        },
91	      };
92	    },
93	  },
94	});
95
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>

```text
1	generator client {
2	  provider = "prisma-client-js"
3	}
4	
5	datasource db {
6	  provider = "mysql"
7	  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
8	}
9	
10	enum StatusSlot {
11	  TERSEDIA
12	  DITEMPAH
13	  DIBATALKAN
14	}
15	
16	model User {
17	  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
18	  email        String   @unique
19	  passwordHash String
20	  nama         String
21	  createdAt    DateTime @default(now())
22	
23	  @@map("users")
24	}
25	
26	model Pelanggan {
27	  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
28	  nama      String
29	  noTelefon String   @unique
30	  nota      String?  @db.Text
31	  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
32	
33	  temujanji Temujanji[]
34	  tempahan  Tempahan[]
35	
36	  @@map("pelanggan")
37	}
38	
39	model SlotTemujanji {
40	  id        String     @id @default(cuid())
41	  tarikh    DateTime   @db.Date
42	  masaMula  String
43	  masaTamat String
44	  status    StatusSlot @default(TERSEDIA)
45	  createdAt DateTime   @default(now())
46	
47	  temujanji Temujanji?
48	
49	  @@map("slot_temujanji")
50	}
51	
52	model Temujanji {
53	  id              String   @id @default(cuid())
54	  pelangganId     String
55	  slotTemujanjiId String   @unique
56	  nota            String?  @db.Text
57	  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())
58	
59	  pelanggan     Pelanggan     @relation(fields: [pelangganId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
60	  slotTemujanji SlotTemujanji @relation(fields: [slotTemujanjiId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
61	
62	  @@map("temujanji")
63	}
64	
65	model SlotTempahan {
66	  id        String     @id @default(cuid())
67	  tarikh    DateTime   @db.Date
68	  status    StatusSlot @default(TERSEDIA)
69	  createdAt DateTime   @default(now())
70	
71	  tempahan Tempahan?
72	
73	  @@map("slot_tempahan")
74	}
75	
76	model Tempahan {
77	  id             String   @id @default(cuid())
78	  namaTempahan   String
79	  slotTempahanId String   @unique
80	  pakejId        String
81	  pelangganId    String
82	  nota           String?  @db.Text
83	  createdAt      DateTime @default(now())
84	
85	  slotTempahan SlotTempahan @relation(fields: [slotTempahanId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
86	  pakej        Pakej        @relation(fields: [pakejId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
87	  pelanggan    Pelanggan    @relation(fields: [pelangganId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
88	
89	  @@map("tempahan")
90	}
91	
92	model Pakej {
93	  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
94	  namaPakej String   @unique
95	  warna     String
96	  gambar    String?
97	  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
98	
99	  tiers    PakejTier[]
100	  sections PakejSection[]
101	  tempahan Tempahan[]
102	
103	  @@map("pakej")
104	}
105	
106	model PakejTier {
107	  id          String   @id @default(cuid())
108	  pakejId     String
109	  namaTier    String
110	  bilanganPax Int
111	  harga       Decimal  @db.Decimal(10, 2)
112	  createdAt   DateTime @default(now())
113	
114	  pakej Pakej @relation(fields: [pakejId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
115	
116	  @@map("pakej_tier")
117	}
118	
119	model PakejSection {
120	  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
121	  pakejId   String
122	  tajuk     String
123	  items     Json
124	  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
125	
126	  pakej Pakej @relation(fields: [pakejId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
127	
128	  @@map("pakej_section")
129	}
130

1	import { PrismaClient } from '@prisma/client';
2	
3	const prisma = new PrismaClient();
4	
5	interface TierData {
6	  readonly namaTier: string;
7	  readonly bilanganPax: number;
8	  readonly harga: number;
9	}
10	
11	interface SectionData {
12	  readonly tajuk: string;
13	  readonly items: readonly string[];
14	}
15	
16	interface PakejData {
17	  readonly namaPakej: string;
18	  readonly warna: string;
19	  readonly tiers: readonly TierData[];
20	  readonly sections: readonly SectionData[];
21	}
22	
23	async function seedAdmin(): Promise<void> {
24	  const email = process.env.ADMIN_EMAIL;
25	  const passwordHash = process.env.ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH;
26	
27	  if (!email || !passwordHash) {
28	    console.log(
29	      'Skipping admin seed: ADMIN_EMAIL or ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH not set.',
30	    );
31	    return;
32	  }
33	
34	  await prisma.user.upsert({
35	    where: { email },
36	    update: { passwordHash, nama: 'Admin' },
37	    create: { email, passwordHash, nama: 'Admin' },
38	  });
39	
40	  console.log(`Admin user upserted: ${email}`);
41	}
42	
43	async function seedPakej(data: PakejData): Promise<void> {
44	  const pakej = await prisma.pakej.upsert({
45	    where: { namaPakej: data.namaPakej },
46	    update: { warna: data.warna },
47	    create: { namaPakej: data.namaPakej, warna: data.warna },
48	  });
49	
50	  // Delete existing tiers and sections, then recreate
51	  await prisma.pakejTier.deleteMany({ where: { pakejId: pakej.id } });
52	  await prisma.pakejSection.deleteMany({ where: { pakejId: pakej.id } });
53	
54	  await prisma.pakejTier.createMany({
55	    data: data.tiers.map((tier) => ({
56	      pakejId: pakej.id,
57	      namaTier: tier.namaTier,
58	      bilanganPax: tier.bilanganPax,
59	      harga: tier.harga,
60	    })),
61	  });
62	
63	  await prisma.pakejSection.createMany({
64	    data: data.sections.map((section) => ({
65	      pakejId: pakej.id,
66	      tajuk: section.tajuk,
67	      items: section.items as unknown as string[],
68	    })),
69	  });
70	
71	  console.log(`Pakej seeded: ${data.namaPakej}`);
72	}

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./app/api/temujanji/route.ts`

```text
1	import { NextRequest } from 'next/server';
2	import { prisma } from '@/lib/db';
3	
4	export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';
5	import {
6	  successResponse,
7	  errorResponse,
8	  withErrorHandling,
9	  validateBody,
10	  createRateLimiter,
11	} from '@/lib/api';
12	import { temujanjiSchema } from '@/lib/validations/temujanji';
13	
14	const rateLimit = createRateLimiter({
15	  interval: 60_000,
16	  maxRequests: 5,
17	});
18	
19	type TransactionError = {
20	  readonly ok: false;
21	  readonly error: string;
22	  readonly status: number;
23	};
24	
25	type TransactionSuccess = {
26	  readonly ok: true;
27	  readonly temujanji: { readonly id: string };
28	  readonly pelanggan: { readonly id: string };
29	};
30	
31	type TransactionResult = TransactionError | TransactionSuccess;
32	
33	export const POST = withErrorHandling(async (req: NextRequest) => {
34	  const rateLimited = rateLimit(req);
35	  if (rateLimited) return rateLimited;
36	
37	  const body: unknown = await req.json();
38	  const data = validateBody(temujanjiSchema, body);
39	
40	  const noTelefonNormalized = data.noTelefon.replace(/-/g, '');
41	
42	  const result: TransactionResult = await prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {
43	    const slot = await tx.slotTemujanji.findUnique({
44	      where: { id: data.slotTemujanjiId },
45	    });
46	
47	    if (!slot) {
48	      return { ok: false, error: 'Slot temujanji tidak dijumpai', status: 404 };
49	    }
50	
51	    if (slot.status === 'DITEMPAH') {
52	      return { ok: false, error: 'Slot ini telah ditempah', status: 409 };
53	    }
54	
55	    const pelanggan = await tx.pelanggan.upsert({
56	      where: { noTelefon: noTelefonNormalized },
57	      update: { nama: data.nama },
58	      create: {
59	        nama: data.nama,
60	        noTelefon: noTelefonNormalized,
61	      },
62	    });
63	
64	    const temujanji = await tx.temujanji.create({
65	      data: {
66	        pelangganId: pelanggan.id,
67	        slotTemujanjiId: data.slotTemujanjiId,
68	        nota: data.nota ?? null,
69	      },
70	    });
71	
72	    await tx.slotTemujanji.update({
73	      where: { id: data.slotTemujanjiId },
74	      data: { status: 'DITEMPAH' },
75	    });
76	
77	    return { ok: true, temujanji, pelanggan };
78	  });
79	
80	  if (!result.ok) {
81	    return errorResponse(result.error, result.status);
82	  }
83	
84	  return successResponse(
85	    { temujanji: result.temujanji, pelanggan: result.pelanggan },
86	    201,
87	  );
88	});
89
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./lib/api/response.ts`

```text
1	import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';
2	
3	type ApiResponse<T> = {
4	  readonly success: boolean;
5	  readonly data: T | null;
6	  readonly error: string | null;
7	};
8	
9	export function successResponse<T>(data: T, status = 200) {
10	  return NextResponse.json<ApiResponse<T>>(
11	    { success: true, data, error: null },
12	    { status },
13	  );
14	}
15	
16	export function errorResponse(message: string, status = 500) {
17	  return NextResponse.json<ApiResponse<null>>(
18	    { success: false, data: null, error: message },
19	    { status },
20	  );
21	}
22
```
</details></tool-use>

6.1 admin layout + login

```text
All files created, type-check passes, build succeeds. Here is a summary of what was built:

## Files Created (7 new files)

### Server Actions
- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/lib/actions/auth.ts` -- `loginAction` (uses `useActionState` pattern, calls `signIn("credentials")`, returns error state on failure, redirects to `/admin` on success) and `logoutAction` (calls `signOut`, redirects to `/admin/login`).

### Admin Layout
- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/app/admin/layout.tsx` -- Server component that calls `auth()` to get the session. When authenticated, wraps children in the `AdminShell` (sidebar + topbar). When no session (i.e., the login page), renders children directly without the shell. This avoids the need for route groups while keeping the login page standalone.

### Login Page
- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/app/admin/login/layout.tsx` -- Minimal pass-through layout with login-specific metadata.
- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/app/admin/login/page.tsx` -- Client component using `useActionState` with `loginAction`. Email + password form in a centered card on cream background. Shows inline error "E-mel atau kata laluan salah" on failure. Uses existing `Button`, `Input`, and `Card` UI components.

### Admin Shell Components
- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/components/admin/admin-shell.tsx` -- Client component that manages sidebar open/close state and composes `Sidebar` + `Topbar` + main content area in a flex layout.
- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/components/admin/sidebar.tsx` -- Client component with `usePathname()` for active nav highlighting. 7 nav items (Dashboard, Slot Temujanji, Temujanji, Slot Tempahan, Tempahan, Pakej, Pelanggan) with Heroicons-style SVG icons. Navy background, responsive: hidden on mobile with overlay toggle, full-width on desktop. Logout button at bottom.
- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/components/admin/topbar.tsx` -- Shows admin name/email, mobile hamburger toggle, and logout button. White background with navy text.

### Dashboard Placeholder
- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/app/admin/page.tsx` -- Simple heading "Dashboard" with welcome text, ready for 6.2 to fill in.

### Key Design Decisions
- The admin layout conditionally renders the shell based on session presence (no session = pass-through for login page), working harmoniously with the existing middleware that allows `/admin/login` without auth.
- Used `useActionState` (React 19) for the login form instead of manual state management -- the Next.js 16 recommended pattern.
- All components use the project's brand colors (navy sidebar, cream login background, white content area) and existing UI primitives.

_**Agent - sidechain (2026-04-20 09:55:41Z)**_

You are working on the MawarBiru project at /home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru. Build the admin layout shell and login page (task 6.1).

IMPORTANT: This is Next.js 16. Read relevant docs from `node_modules/next/dist/docs/` if you're unsure about any conventions. Check for deprecation notices.

## What already exists
- **Auth**: `/lib/auth.ts` — NextAuth.js with CredentialsProvider, JWT strategy. Exports `{ handlers, auth, signIn, signOut }`. Session has `user.id`, `user.nama`, `user.email`.
- **Middleware**: `/middleware.ts` — protects `/admin/*` routes, redirects unauthenticated to `/admin/login`, returns 401 JSON for `/api/admin/*`
- **Prisma schema**: User model with `id`, `email`, `passwordHash`, `nama`
- **Seed**: Creates admin user from ADMIN_EMAIL + ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH env vars
- **API route handler at `/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts`** — should already exist (check first)
- **UI components**: Button, Input, Label, Card, Badge, Spinner available from `@/components/ui`
- **API helpers**: `successResponse`, `errorResponse`, `withErrorHandling` from `@/lib/api`
- **Design**: Tailwind v4 with CSS variables in globals.css. Brand colors: navy (#07203F), cream (#EBDED4), dusty-rose (#D9AA90), terracotta (#A65E46)

## Files to create

### 1. `app/admin/login/page.tsx`
- Email + password form
- Use server action that calls `signIn("credentials", ...)` from next-auth
- On success: redirect to `/admin`
- On failure: show inline error "E-mel atau kata laluan salah"
- Styled with brand colors — simple centered card on cream background
- NOT wrapped in admin layout (standalone page)

### 2. `app/admin/login/layout.tsx`
- Minimal layout for the login page (just renders children, no sidebar)
- Includes the html structure needed for standalone page

### 3. `app/admin/layout.tsx`
- Admin shell layout wrapping all admin routes EXCEPT login
- Collapsible sidebar with nav links:
  - Dashboard (`/admin`)
  - Slot Temujanji (`/admin/slot-temujanji`)
  - Temujanji (`/admin/temujanji`)
  - Slot Tempahan (`/admin/slot-tempahan`)
  - Tempahan (`/admin/tempahan`)
  - Pakej (`/admin/pakej`)
  - Pelanggan (`/admin/pelanggan`)
- Top bar showing admin name/email + logout button
- Active nav item highlighting based on current pathname
- Sidebar collapses to icons on tablet (responsive)
- Use `auth()` from `@/lib/auth` to get session server-side
- If no session, redirect to login (defensive, middleware should catch this)

### 4. `app/admin/page.tsx`
- Simple placeholder dashboard page (just a heading "Dashboard" for now — 6.2 fills this in later)

### 5. `components/admin/sidebar.tsx`
- Client component (needs `usePathname()` for active state)
- Collapsible: full width on desktop, icon-only on tablet, hidden on mobile with toggle
- Nav items with icons (use simple SVG icons inline or emoji placeholders)
- Logout button at bottom of sidebar

### 6. `components/admin/topbar.tsx`
- Shows admin name from session
- Mobile sidebar toggle button
- Logout button (calls signOut action)

### 7. `lib/actions/auth.ts`
- Server actions for login and logout
- `loginAction(formData)` — calls signIn, handles errors
- `logoutAction()` — calls signOut, redirects to /admin/login

## Technical notes
- Check if `/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts` exists. If not, create it exporting `handlers.GET` and `handlers.POST` from `@/lib/auth`.
- The admin layout should use a flex layout: sidebar on left, main content on right
- Use `"use client"` only where needed (sidebar, topbar for interactivity)
- Style consistently with the project's Tailwind classes
- Admin pages use a white/light background for the content area, navy sidebar
- Keep it functional — this is standard rigor, not luxury client-facing

```text
1	import NextAuth from "next-auth";
2	import Credentials from "next-auth/providers/credentials";
3	import { compare } from "bcryptjs";
4	import { prisma } from "@/lib/db";
5	
6	declare module "next-auth" {
7	  interface Session {
8	    user: {
9	      id: string;
10	      nama: string;
11	      email: string;
12	    };
13	  }
14	
15	  interface User {
16	    nama: string;
17	  }
18	}
19	
20	declare module "@auth/core/jwt" {
21	  interface JWT {
22	    id: string;
23	    nama: string;
24	  }
25	}
26	
27	export const { handlers, auth, signIn, signOut } = NextAuth({
28	  secret: process.env.NEXTAUTH_SECRET,
29	  session: {
30	    strategy: "jwt",
31	    maxAge: 24 * 60 * 60, // 24 hours
32	  },
33	  pages: {
34	    signIn: "/admin/login",
35	  },
36	  providers: [
37	    Credentials({
38	      name: "credentials",
39	      credentials: {
40	        email: { label: "E-mel", type: "email" },
41	        password: { label: "Kata Laluan", type: "password" },
42	      },
43	      async authorize(credentials) {
44	        const email =
45	          typeof credentials?.email === "string" ? credentials.email : "";
46	        const password =
47	          typeof credentials?.password === "string" ? credentials.password : "";
48	
49	        if (!email || !password) {
50	          throw new Error("E-mel atau kata laluan tidak sah");
51	        }
52	
53	        const user = await prisma.user.findUnique({
54	          where: { email },
55	        });
56	
57	        if (!user) {
58	          throw new Error("E-mel atau kata laluan tidak sah");
59	        }
60	
61	        const isPasswordValid = await compare(password, user.passwordHash);
62	
63	        if (!isPasswordValid) {
64	          throw new Error("E-mel atau kata laluan tidak sah");
65	        }
66	
67	        return {
68	          id: user.id,
69	          email: user.email,
70	          nama: user.nama,
71	        };
72	      },
73	    }),
74	  ],
75	  callbacks: {
76	    jwt({ token, user }) {
77	      if (user) {
78	        token.id = user.id as string;
79	        token.nama = (user as { nama: string }).nama;
80	      }
81	      return token;
82	    },
83	    session({ session, token }) {
84	      return {
85	        ...session,
86	        user: {
87	          ...session.user,
88	          id: token.id,
89	          nama: token.nama,
90	        },
91	      };
92	    },
93	  },
94	});
95
```
</details></tool-use>

```text
1	import { auth } from "@/lib/auth";
2	import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
3	
4	export default auth((req) => {
5	  const { pathname } = req.nextUrl;
6	
7	  // Allow access to the login page without authentication
8	  if (pathname === "/admin/login") {
9	    return NextResponse.next();
10	  }
11	
12	  // Protect all /admin/* and /api/admin/* routes
13	  if (!req.auth) {
14	    // API routes return JSON 401 instead of redirecting
15	    if (pathname.startsWith("/api/admin")) {
16	      return NextResponse.json(
17	        { error: "Tidak dibenarkan" },
18	        { status: 401 },
19	      );
20	    }
21	
22	    const loginUrl = new URL("/admin/login", req.nextUrl.origin);
23	    loginUrl.searchParams.set("callbackUrl", pathname);
24	    return NextResponse.redirect(loginUrl);
25	  }
26	
27	  return NextResponse.next();
28	});
29	
30	export const config = {
31	  matcher: ["/admin/:path*", "/api/admin/:path*"],
32	};
33
```
</details></tool-use>

3	import { forwardRef, type ButtonHTMLAttributes } from 'react';

5	import { cn } from '@/lib/utils';
6	
7	const buttonVariants = cva(
8	  [
9	    'inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2',
10	    'font-sans font-medium',
11	    'rounded-button',
12	    'transition-colors duration-150',
13	    'motion-reduce:transition-none',
14	    'focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-2 focus-visible:ring-dusty-rose',
15	    'active:scale-[0.98]',
16	    'disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50',
17	  ],
18	  {
19	    variants: {
20	      variant: {
21	        primary: 'bg-navy text-cream hover:bg-navy-light active:bg-navy-dark',
22	        secondary:
23	          'bg-cream text-navy border border-navy hover:bg-cream-dark active:bg-cream-dark',
24	        accent:
25	          'bg-dusty-rose text-navy hover:bg-dusty-rose-light active:bg-dusty-rose-dark',
26	        ghost: 'bg-transparent text-navy hover:bg-cream-dark active:bg-cream-dark',
27	        danger: 'bg-red-600 text-white hover:bg-red-700 active:bg-red-800',
28	      },
29	      size: {
30	        sm: 'h-8 px-3 text-sm',
31	        md: 'h-10 px-4 text-base',
32	        lg: 'h-12 px-6 text-lg',
33	      },
34	    },
35	    defaultVariants: {
36	      variant: 'primary',
37	      size: 'md',
38	    },
39	  },
40	);
41	
42	type ButtonProps = ButtonHTMLAttributes<HTMLButtonElement> &
43	  VariantProps<typeof buttonVariants> & {
44	    loading?: boolean;
45	    'data-testid'?: string;
46	  };
47	
48	const Button = forwardRef<HTMLButtonElement, ButtonProps>(
49	  (
50	    {
51	      className,
52	      variant,
53	      size,
54	      loading = false,
55	      disabled,
56	      children,
57	      ...props
58	    },
59	    ref,
60	  ) => {
61	    return (
62	      <button
63	        ref={ref}
64	        className={cn(buttonVariants({ variant, size }), className)}
65	        disabled={disabled || loading}
66	        aria-busy={loading || undefined}
67	        {...props}
68	      >
69	        {loading && <Spinner />}
70	        {children}
71	      </button>
72	    );
73	  },
74	);
75	
76	Button.displayName = 'Button';
77	
78	function Spinner() {
79	  return (
80	    <svg
81	      className="h-4 w-4 animate-spin"
82	      xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
83	      fill="none"
84	      viewBox="0 0 24 24"
85	      aria-hidden="true"
86	    >
87	      <circle
88	        className="opacity-25"
89	        cx="12"
90	        cy="12"
91	        r="10"
92	        stroke="currentColor"
93	        strokeWidth="4"
94	      />
95	      <path
96	        className="opacity-75"
97	        fill="currentColor"
98	        d="M4 12a8 8 0 018-8V0C5.373 0 0 5.373 0 12h4z"
99	      />
100	    </svg>
101	  );
102	}
103	
104	export { Button, buttonVariants };
105	export type { ButtonProps };
106

5	import { Label } from './label';
6	
7	type InputProps = InputHTMLAttributes<HTMLInputElement> & {
8	  label?: string;
9	  error?: string;
10	  helperText?: string;
11	  'data-testid'?: string;
12	};
13	
14	const Input = forwardRef<HTMLInputElement, InputProps>(
15	  ({ className, label, error, helperText, id, required, ...props }, ref) => {
16	    const generatedId = useId();
17	    const inputId = id ?? generatedId;
18	    const errorId = error ? `${inputId}-error` : undefined;
19	    const helperId = helperText ? `${inputId}-helper` : undefined;
20	    const describedBy = [errorId, helperId].filter(Boolean).join(' ') || undefined;
21	
22	    return (
23	      <div className="flex flex-col gap-1.5">
24	        {label && (
25	          <Label htmlFor={inputId} required={required}>
26	            {label}
27	          </Label>
28	        )}
29	        <input
30	          ref={ref}
31	          id={inputId}
32	          required={required}
33	          aria-invalid={error ? true : undefined}
34	          aria-describedby={describedBy}
35	          className={cn(
36	            'rounded-button border bg-cream px-3 py-2 text-base text-navy',
37	            'transition-colors duration-150',
38	            'motion-reduce:transition-none',
39	            'placeholder:text-navy/50',
40	            'focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-dusty-rose focus-visible:ring-offset-2',
41	            'disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50',
42	            error
43	              ? 'border-red-600 focus-visible:ring-red-600'
44	              : 'border-navy',
45	            className,
46	          )}
47	          {...props}
48	        />
49	        {error && (
50	          <p id={errorId} className="text-sm text-red-600" role="alert">
51	            {error}
52	          </p>
53	        )}
54	        {helperText && !error && (
55	          <p id={helperId} className="text-sm text-navy/60">
56	            {helperText}
57	          </p>
58	        )}
59	      </div>
60	    );
61	  },
62	);
63	
64	Input.displayName = 'Input';
65	
66	export { Input };
67	export type { InputProps };
68
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>

 `./node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/02-guides/authentication.md`

```text
1	---
2	title: How to implement authentication in Next.js
3	nav_title: Authentication
4	description: Learn how to implement authentication in your Next.js application.
5	---
6	
7	Understanding authentication is crucial for protecting your application's data. This page will guide you through what React and Next.js features to use to implement auth.
8	
9	Before starting, it helps to break down the process into three concepts:
10	
11	1. **[Authentication](#authentication)**: Verifies if the user is who they say they are. It requires the user to prove their identity with something they have, such as a username and password.
12	2. **[Session Management](#session-management)**: Tracks the user's auth state across requests.
13	3. **[Authorization](#authorization)**: Decides what routes and data the user can access.
14	
15	This diagram shows the authentication flow using React and Next.js features:
16	
17	<Image
18	  alt="Diagram showing the authentication flow with React and Next.js features"
19	  srcLight="/docs/light/authentication-overview.png"
20	  srcDark="/docs/dark/authentication-overview.png"
21	  width="1600"
22	  height="1383"
23	/>
24	
25	The examples on this page walk through basic username and password auth for educational purposes. While you can implement a custom auth solution, for increased security and simplicity, we recommend using an authentication library. These offer built-in solutions for authentication, session management, and authorization, as well as additional features such as social logins, multi-factor authentication, and role-based access control. You can find a list in the [Auth Libraries](#auth-libraries) section.
26	
27	## Authentication
28	
29	<AppOnly>
30	
31	### Sign-up and login functionality
32	
33	You can use the [`<form>`](https://react.dev/reference/react-dom/components/form) element with React's [Server Actions](/docs/app/getting-started/mutating-data) and `useActionState` to capture user credentials, validate form fields, and call your Authentication Provider's API or database.
34	
35	Since Server Actions always execute on the server, they provide a secure environment for handling authentication logic.
36	
37	Here are the steps to implement signup/login functionality:
38	
39	#### 1. Capture user credentials
40	
41	To capture user credentials, create a form that invokes a Server Action on submission. For example, a signup form that accepts the user's name, email, and password:
42	
43	```tsx filename="app/ui/signup-form.tsx" switcher
44	import { signup } from '@/app/actions/auth'
45	
46	export function SignupForm() {
47	  return (
48	    <form action={signup}>
49	      <div>
50	        <label htmlFor="name">Name</label>
51	        <input id="name" name="name" placeholder="Name" />
52	      </div>
53	      <div>
54	        <label htmlFor="email">Email</label>
55	        <input id="email" name="email" type="email" placeholder="Email" />
56	      </div>
57	      <div>
58	        <label htmlFor="password">Password</label>
59	        <input id="password" name="password" type="password" />
60	      </div>
61	      <button type="submit">Sign Up</button>
62	    </form>
63	  )
64	}
65	```
66	
67	```jsx filename="app/ui/signup-form.js" switcher
68	import { signup } from '@/app/actions/auth'
69	
70	export function SignupForm() {
71	  return (
72	    <form action={signup}>
73	      <div>
74	        <label htmlFor="name">Name</label>
75	        <input id="name" name="name" placeholder="Name" />
76	      </div>
77	      <div>
78	        <label htmlFor="email">Email</label>
79	        <input id="email" name="email" type="email" placeholder="Email" />
80	      </div>
81	      <div>
82	        <label htmlFor="password">Password</label>
83	        <input id="password" name="password" type="password" />
84	      </div>
85	      <button type="submit">Sign Up</button>
86	    </form>
87	  )
88	}
89	```
90	
91	```tsx filename="app/actions/auth.ts" switcher
92	export async function signup(formData: FormData) {}
93	```
94	
95	```jsx filename="app/actions/auth.js" switcher
96	export async function signup(formData) {}
97	```
98	
99	#### 2. Validate form fields on the server
100
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-20 09:56:15Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/02-guides/forms.md`

```text
1	---
2	title: How to create forms with Server Actions
3	nav_title: Forms
4	description: Learn how to create forms in Next.js with React Server Actions.
5	---
6	
7	React Server Actions are [Server Functions](https://react.dev/reference/rsc/server-functions) that execute on the server. They can be called in Server and Client Components to handle form submissions. This guide will walk you through how to create forms in Next.js with Server Actions.
8	
9	> [!WARNING]
10	> Always verify [authentication and authorization](/docs/app/guides/authentication) inside each Server Action, even if the form is only rendered on an authenticated page. See the [Data Security guide](/docs/app/guides/data-security) for more details.
11	
12	## How it works
13	
14	React extends the HTML [`<form>`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/HTML/Element/form) element to allow Server Actions to be invoked with the [`action`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/form#action) attribute.
15	
16	When used in a form, the function automatically receives the [`FormData`](https://developer.mozilla.org/docs/Web/API/FormData/FormData) object. You can then extract the data using the native [`FormData` methods](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/FormData#instance_methods):
17	
18	```tsx filename="app/invoices/page.tsx" switcher
19	import { auth } from '@/lib/auth'
20	
21	export default function Page() {
22	  async function createInvoice(formData: FormData) {
23	    'use server'
24	
25	    const session = await auth()
26	    if (!session?.user) {
27	      throw new Error('Unauthorized')
28	    }
29	
30	    const rawFormData = {
31	      customerId: formData.get('customerId'),
32	      amount: formData.get('amount'),
33	      status: formData.get('status'),
34	    }
35	
36	    // mutate data
37	    // revalidate the cache
38	  }
39	
40	  return <form action={createInvoice}>...</form>
41	}
42	```
43	
44	```jsx filename="app/invoices/page.js" switcher
45	import { auth } from '@/lib/auth'
46	
47	export default function Page() {
48	  async function createInvoice(formData) {
49	    'use server'
50	
51	    const session = await auth()
52	    if (!session?.user) {
53	      throw new Error('Unauthorized')
54	    }
55	
56	    const rawFormData = {
57	      customerId: formData.get('customerId'),
58	      amount: formData.get('amount'),
59	      status: formData.get('status'),
60	    }
61	
62	    // mutate data
63	    // revalidate the cache
64	  }
65	
66	  return <form action={createInvoice}>...</form>
67	}
68	```
69	
70	> **Good to know:** When working with forms that have multiple fields, use JavaScript's [`Object.fromEntries()`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Object/fromEntries). For example: `const rawFormData = Object.fromEntries(formData)`. Note that this object will contain extra properties prefixed with `$ACTION_`.
71	
72	## Passing additional arguments
73	
74	Outside of form fields, you can pass additional arguments to a Server Function using the JavaScript [`bind`](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Function/bind) method. For example, to pass the `userId` argument to the `updateUser` Server Function:
75	
76	```tsx filename="app/client-component.tsx" highlight={6} switcher
77	'use client'
78	
79	import { updateUser } from './actions'
80	
81	export function UserProfile({ userId }: { userId: string }) {
82	  const updateUserWithId = updateUser.bind(null, userId)
83	
84	  return (
85	    <form action={updateUserWithId}>
86	      <input type="text" name="name" />
87	      <button type="submit">Update User Name</button>
88	    </form>
89	  )
90	}
91	```
92	
93	```jsx filename="app/client-component.js" highlight={6} switcher
94	'use client'
95	
96	import { updateUser } from './actions'
97	
98	export function UserProfile({ userId }) {
99	  const updateUserWithId = updateUser.bind(null, userId)
100
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-20 09:56:15Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/02-guides/redirecting.md`

```text
1	---
2	title: How to handle redirects in Next.js
3	nav_title: Redirecting
4	description: Learn the different ways to handle redirects in Next.js.
5	related:
6	  links:
7	    - app/api-reference/functions/redirect
8	    - app/api-reference/functions/permanentRedirect
9	    - app/api-reference/file-conventions/proxy
10	    - app/api-reference/config/next-config-js/redirects
11	---
12	
13	There are a few ways you can handle redirects in Next.js. This page will go through each available option, use cases, and how to manage large numbers of redirects.
14	
15	<AppOnly>
16	
17	| API                                                           | Purpose                                           | Where                                               | Status Code                            |
18	| ------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------- |
19	| [`redirect`](#redirect-function)                              | Redirect user after a mutation or event           | Server Components, Server Functions, Route Handlers | 307 (Temporary) or 303 (Server Action) |
20	| [`permanentRedirect`](#permanentredirect-function)            | Redirect user after a mutation or event           | Server Components, Server Functions, Route Handlers | 308 (Permanent)                        |
21	| [`useRouter`](#userouter-hook)                                | Perform a client-side navigation                  | Event Handlers in Client Components                 | N/A                                    |
22	| [`redirects` in `next.config.js`](#redirects-in-nextconfigjs) | Redirect an incoming request based on a path      | `next.config.js` file                               | 307 (Temporary) or 308 (Permanent)     |
23	| [`NextResponse.redirect`](#nextresponseredirect-in-proxy)     | Redirect an incoming request based on a condition | Proxy                                               | Any                                    |
24	
25	</AppOnly>
26	
27	<PagesOnly>
28	
29	| API                                                           | Purpose                                           | Where                 | Status Code                        |
30	| ------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | --------------------- | ---------------------------------- |
31	| [`useRouter`](#userouter-hook)                                | Perform a client-side navigation                  | Components            | N/A                                |
32	| [`redirects` in `next.config.js`](#redirects-in-nextconfigjs) | Redirect an incoming request based on a path      | `next.config.js` file | 307 (Temporary) or 308 (Permanent) |
33	| [`NextResponse.redirect`](#nextresponseredirect-in-proxy)     | Redirect an incoming request based on a condition | Proxy                 | Any                                |
34	
35	</PagesOnly>
36	
37	<AppOnly>
38	
39	## `redirect` function
40	
41	The `redirect` function allows you to redirect the user to another URL. You can call `redirect` in [Server Components](/docs/app/getting-started/server-and-client-components), [Route Handlers](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/route), and [Server Functions](/docs/app/getting-started/mutating-data).
42	
43	`redirect` is often used after a mutation or event. For example, creating a post:
44	
45	```ts filename="app/actions.ts" switcher
46	'use server'
47	
48	import { redirect } from 'next/navigation'
49	import { revalidatePath } from 'next/cache'
50	
51	export async function createPost(id: string) {
52	  try {
53	    // Call database
54	  } catch (error) {
55	    // Handle errors
56	  }
57	
58	  revalidatePath('/posts') // Update cached posts
59	  redirect(`/post/${id}`) // Navigate to the new post page
60	}
61	```
62	
63	```js filename="app/actions.js" switcher
64	'use server'
65	
66	import { redirect } from 'next/navigation'
67	import { revalidatePath } from 'next/cache'
68	
69	export async function createPost(id) {
70	  try {
71	    // Call database
72	  } catch (error) {
73	    // Handle errors
74	  }
75	
76	  revalidatePath('/posts') // Update cached posts
77	  redirect(`/post/${id}`) // Navigate to the new post page
78	}
79	```
80
```
</details></tool-use>

<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/layout.md`

```text
1	---
2	title: layout.js
3	description: API reference for the layout.js file.
4	---
5	
6	The `layout` file is used to define a layout in your Next.js application.
7	
8	```tsx filename="app/dashboard/layout.tsx" switcher
9	export default function DashboardLayout({
10	  children,
11	}: {
12	  children: React.ReactNode
13	}) {
14	  return <section>{children}</section>
15	}
16	```
17	
18	```jsx filename="app/dashboard/layout.js" switcher
19	export default function DashboardLayout({ children }) {
20	  return <section>{children}</section>
21	}
22	```
23	
24	In the [component hierarchy](/docs/app/getting-started/project-structure#component-hierarchy), `layout.js` is the outermost component in a route segment. It wraps `template.js`, `error.js`, `loading.js`, `not-found.js`, and `page.js`.
25	
26	A **root layout** is the top-most layout in the root `app` directory. It is used to define the `<html>` and `<body>` tags and other globally shared UI.
27	
28	```tsx filename="app/layout.tsx" switcher
29	export default function RootLayout({
30	  children,
31	}: {
32	  children: React.ReactNode
33	}) {
34	  return (
35	    <html lang="en">
36	      <body>{children}</body>
37	    </html>
38	  )
39	}
40	```
41	
42	```jsx filename="app/layout.js" switcher
43	export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
44	  return (
45	    <html lang="en">
46	      <body>{children}</body>
47	    </html>
48	  )
49	}
50	```
51	
52	## Reference
53	
54	### Props
55	
56	#### `children` (required)
57	
58	Layout components should accept and use a `children` prop. During rendering, `children` will be populated with the route segments the layout is wrapping. These will primarily be the component of a child [Layout](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/page) (if it exists) or [Page](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/page), but could also be other special files like [Loading](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/loading) or [Error](/docs/app/getting-started/error-handling) when applicable.
59	
60	#### `params` (optional)
61	
62	A promise that resolves to an object containing the [dynamic route parameters](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/dynamic-routes) object from the root segment down to that layout.
63	
64	```tsx filename="app/dashboard/[team]/layout.tsx" switcher
65	export default async function Layout({
66	  children,
67	  params,
68	}: {
69	  children: React.ReactNode
70	  params: Promise<{ team: string }>
71	}) {
72	  const { team } = await params
73	}
74	```
75	
76	```jsx filename="app/dashboard/[team]/layout.js" switcher
77	export default async function Layout({ children, params }) {
78	  const { team } = await params
79	}
80	```
```
</details></tool-use>

Good, I have a clear picture. Now let me check the route-groups convention to understand how the login page can have a separate layout.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-20 09:56:35Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/03-api-reference/03-file-conventions/route-groups.md`

```text
1	---
2	title: Route Groups
3	description: Route Groups can be used to partition your Next.js application into different sections.
4	---
5	
6	Route Groups are a folder convention that let you organize routes by category or team.
7	
8	## Convention
9	
10	A route group can be created by wrapping a folder's name in parenthesis: `(folderName)`.
11	
12	This convention indicates the folder is for organizational purposes and should **not be included** in the route's URL path.
13	
14	<Image
15	  alt="An example folder structure using route groups"
16	  srcLight="/docs/light/project-organization-route-groups.png"
17	  srcDark="/docs/dark/project-organization-route-groups.png"
18	  width="1600"
19	  height="849"
20	/>
21	
22	## Use cases
23	
24	- Organizing routes by team, concern, or feature.
25	- Defining multiple [root layouts](/docs/app/api-reference/file-conventions/layout#root-layout).
26	- Opting specific route segments into sharing a layout, while keeping others out.
27	
28	## Caveats
29	
30	- **Full page load**: If you navigate between routes that use different root layouts, it'll trigger a full page reload. For example, navigating from `/cart` that uses `app/(shop)/layout.js` to `/blog` that uses `app/(marketing)/layout.js`. This **only** applies to multiple root layouts.
31	- **Conflicting paths**: Routes in different groups should not resolve to the same URL path. For example, `(marketing)/about/page.js` and `(shop)/about/page.js` would both resolve to `/about` and cause an error.
32	- **Top-level root layout**: If you use multiple root layouts without a top-level `layout.js` file, make sure your home route (/) is defined within one of the route groups, e.g. app/(marketing)/page.js.
33
```
</details></tool-use>

The best approach with Next.js: The `app/admin/layout.tsx` wraps everything. The login page has its own nested `layout.tsx`. The admin layout should check for session and only show the sidebar/topbar shell when authenticated. When not authenticated (login page), it just passes children through.

```typescript
"use server";

import { signIn, signOut } from "@/lib/auth";
import { redirect } from "next/navigation";
import { AuthError } from "next-auth";

interface LoginActionState {
  error: string | null;
}

export async function loginAction(
  _prevState: LoginActionState,
  formData: FormData,
): Promise<LoginActionState> {
  try {
    await signIn("credentials", {
      email: formData.get("email") as string,
      password: formData.get("password") as string,
      redirect: false,
    });
  } catch (error: unknown) {
    if (error instanceof AuthError) {
      return { error: "E-mel atau kata laluan salah" };
    }
    throw error;
  }

  redirect("/admin");
}

export async function logoutAction(): Promise<void> {
  await signOut({ redirectTo: "/admin/login" });
}

```

interface SidebarProps {
  readonly isOpen: boolean;
  readonly onClose: () => void;
}

function isActiveRoute(pathname: string, href: string): boolean {
  if (href === "/admin") {
    return pathname === "/admin";
  }
  return pathname.startsWith(href);
}

export function Sidebar({ isOpen, onClose }: SidebarProps) {
  const pathname = usePathname();

  return (
    <>
      {/* Mobile overlay */}
      {isOpen && (
        <div
          className="fixed inset-0 z-40 bg-black/50 lg:hidden"
          onClick={onClose}
          aria-hidden="true"
        />
      )}

      {/* Sidebar */}
      <aside
        className={cn(
          "fixed top-0 left-0 z-50 flex h-full w-64 flex-col bg-navy text-cream transition-transform duration-200 lg:static lg:z-auto lg:translate-x-0",
          "md:w-64 lg:w-64",
          isOpen ? "translate-x-0" : "-translate-x-full",
        )}
      >
        {/* Logo / Brand */}
        <div className="flex h-16 shrink-0 items-center gap-3 border-b border-cream/10 px-4">
          <span className="text-lg font-semibold tracking-tight text-cream">
            MawarBiru
          </span>
          <button
            type="button"
            className="ml-auto text-cream/60 hover:text-cream lg:hidden"
            onClick={onClose}
            aria-label="Tutup menu"
          >
            <svg
              className="h-5 w-5"
              fill="none"
              viewBox="0 0 24 24"
              strokeWidth={1.5}
              stroke="currentColor"
              aria-hidden="true"
            >
              <path
                strokeLinecap="round"
                strokeLinejoin="round"
                d="M6 18 18 6M6 6l12 12"
              />
            </svg>
          </button>
        </div>

        {/* Navigation */}
        <nav className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto px-3 py-4" aria-label="Admin">
          <ul className="flex flex-col gap-1">
            {NAV_ITEMS.map((item) => {
              const active = isActiveRoute(pathname, item.href);
              return (
                <li key={item.href}>
                  <a
                    href={item.href}
                    className={cn(
                      "flex items-center gap-3 rounded-lg px-3 py-2.5 text-sm font-medium transition-colors",
                      active
                        ? "bg-cream/15 text-cream"
                        : "text-cream/70 hover:bg-cream/10 hover:text-cream",
                    )}
                    aria-current={active ? "page" : undefined}
                  >
                    {item.icon}
                    <span>{item.label}</span>
                  </a>
                </li>
              );
            })}
          </ul>
        </nav>

        {/* Logout */}
        <div className="border-t border-cream/10 px-3 py-4">
          <form action={logoutAction}>
            <button
              type="submit"
              className="flex w-full items-center gap-3 rounded-lg px-3 py-2.5 text-sm font-medium text-cream/70 transition-colors hover:bg-cream/10 hover:text-cream"
            >
              <svg
                className="h-5 w-5"
                fill="none"
                viewBox="0 0 24 24"
                strokeWidth={1.5}
                stroke="currentColor"
                aria-hidden="true"
              >
                <path
                  strokeLinecap="round"
                  strokeLinejoin="round"
                  d="M15.75 9V5.25A2.25 2.25 0 0 0 13.5 3h-6a2.25 2.25 0 0 0-2.25 2.25v13.5A2.25 2.25 0 0 0 7.5 21h6a2.25 2.25 0 0 0 2.25-2.25V15m3 0 3-3m0 0-3-3m3 3H9"
                />
              </svg>
              <span>Log Keluar</span>
            </button>
          </form>
        </div>
      </aside>
    </>
  );
}

```

"use client";

import { logoutAction } from "@/lib/actions/auth";

interface TopbarProps {
  readonly nama: string;
  readonly email: string;
  readonly onMenuToggle: () => void;
}

export function Topbar({ nama, email, onMenuToggle }: TopbarProps) {
  return (
    <header className="flex h-16 shrink-0 items-center gap-4 border-b border-navy/10 bg-white px-4 lg:px-6">
      {/* Mobile menu toggle */}
      <button
        type="button"
        className="text-navy/60 hover:text-navy lg:hidden"
        onClick={onMenuToggle}
        aria-label="Buka menu"
      >
        <svg
          className="h-6 w-6"
          fill="none"
          viewBox="0 0 24 24"
          strokeWidth={1.5}
          stroke="currentColor"
          aria-hidden="true"
        >
          <path
            strokeLinecap="round"
            strokeLinejoin="round"
            d="M3.75 6.75h16.5M3.75 12h16.5m-16.5 5.25h16.5"
          />
        </svg>
      </button>

      {/* Spacer */}
      <div className="flex-1" />

      {/* User info + logout */}
      <div className="flex items-center gap-4">
        <div className="hidden text-right sm:block">
          <p className="text-sm font-medium text-navy">{nama}</p>
          <p className="text-xs text-navy/60">{email}</p>
        </div>

        <form action={logoutAction}>
          <button
            type="submit"
            className="rounded-lg px-3 py-1.5 text-sm font-medium text-navy/70 transition-colors hover:bg-navy/5 hover:text-navy"
          >
            Log Keluar
          </button>
        </form>
      </div>
    </header>
  );
}

```tsx
"use client";

import { useState } from "react";
import { Sidebar } from "./sidebar";
import { Topbar } from "./topbar";

interface AdminShellProps {
  readonly nama: string;
  readonly email: string;
  readonly children: React.ReactNode;
}

export function AdminShell({ nama, email, children }: AdminShellProps) {
  const [sidebarOpen, setSidebarOpen] = useState(false);

  return (
    <div className="flex h-screen overflow-hidden bg-gray-50">
      <Sidebar
        isOpen={sidebarOpen}
        onClose={() => setSidebarOpen(false)}
      />

      <div className="flex flex-1 flex-col overflow-hidden">
        <Topbar
          nama={nama}
          email={email}
          onMenuToggle={() => setSidebarOpen((prev) => !prev)}
        />

        <main className="flex-1 overflow-y-auto p-4 lg:p-6">
          {children}
        </main>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

```

```tsx
import { auth } from "@/lib/auth";
import { redirect } from "next/navigation";
import { AdminShell } from "@/components/admin/admin-shell";

export const metadata = {
  title: "Admin - MawarBiru",
};

export default async function AdminLayout({
  children,
}: {
  children: React.ReactNode;
}) {
  const session = await auth();

  if (!session?.user) {
    redirect("/admin/login");
  }

  return (
    <AdminShell nama={session.user.nama} email={session.user.email}>
      {children}
    </AdminShell>
  );
}

```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-20 09:58:13Z)**_

Now the login page. There's an issue: `app/admin/layout.tsx` wraps ALL admin routes including `/admin/login`. If the login layout is nested under admin layout, the admin layout will redirect unauthenticated users -- defeating the purpose of the login page.

I need to restructure this. The proper approach is to use route groups so login gets a different layout from the rest of admin. Let me restructure:

- `app/admin/login/` stays as-is (it's a route at `/admin/login`)
- The admin layout needs to NOT wrap the login page

The way to handle this in Next.js: the middleware already allows `/admin/login` through without auth. But the `app/admin/layout.tsx` would still apply to `/admin/login` since it's nested.

I need to check if the login page can avoid the admin layout redirect. The middleware allows the login page through, but the server component `auth()` check in the admin layout will redirect.

Best solution: move admin authenticated routes into a route group. Let me restructure:

```
app/admin/login/       - login page (no admin shell)
app/admin/(dashboard)/ - all other admin pages with the shell layout
```

Wait, but the task spec says to create `app/admin/layout.tsx` and `app/admin/login/layout.tsx`. Let me re-read...

The task says "NOT wrapped in admin layout (standalone page)" for the login page. The simplest way: make the admin layout check if the path is login and skip the shell. But that's messy.

Better: I'll make the admin layout pass children through without the shell if there's no session (which is the case for the login page). The middleware already allows `/admin/login` without auth, so the admin layout will see no session for the login page and just render children directly.

Let me update the admin layout:

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-20 09:58:16Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
import { auth } from "@/lib/auth";
import { AdminShell } from "@/components/admin/admin-shell";

export const metadata = {
  title: "Admin - MawarBiru",
};

export default async function AdminLayout({
  children,
}: {
  children: React.ReactNode;
}) {
  const session = await auth();

  // When there is no session (e.g. login page), render children without
  // the admin shell. The middleware already handles redirects for protected
  // routes, so this is only reached for public admin routes like /admin/login.
  if (!session?.user) {
    return <>{children}</>;
  }

  return (
    <AdminShell nama={session.user.nama} email={session.user.email}>
      {children}
    </AdminShell>
  );
}

```

</details></tool-use>

Now create the login page. I'll use `useActionState` for the form since that's the React 19 / Next.js 16 way.

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-20 09:58:27Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
"use client";

import { useActionState } from "react";
import { loginAction } from "@/lib/actions/auth";
import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button";
import { Input } from "@/components/ui/input";
import { Card, CardHeader, CardBody, CardFooter } from "@/components/ui/card";

export default function LoginPage() {
  const [state, formAction, isPending] = useActionState(loginAction, {
    error: null,
  });

  return (
    <div className="flex min-h-screen items-center justify-center bg-cream px-4">
      <div className="w-full max-w-sm">
        {/* Brand */}
        <div className="mb-8 text-center">
          <h1 className="font-serif text-3xl font-bold text-navy">MawarBiru</h1>
          <p className="mt-1 text-sm text-navy/60">Panel Pentadbir</p>
        </div>

        <Card className="border border-navy/10 bg-white shadow-elevated">
          <CardHeader>
            <h2 className="text-lg font-semibold text-navy">Log Masuk</h2>
          </CardHeader>

          <form action={formAction}>
            <CardBody className="flex flex-col gap-4">
              {state.error && (
                <div
                  className="rounded-lg border border-red-200 bg-red-50 px-4 py-3 text-sm text-red-700"
                  role="alert"
                >
                  {state.error}
                </div>
              )}

              <Input
                label="E-mel"
                name="email"
                type="email"
                required
                autoComplete="email"
                placeholder="admin@mawarbiru.com"
              />

              <Input
                label="Kata Laluan"
                name="password"
                type="password"
                required
                autoComplete="current-password"
                placeholder="Masukkan kata laluan"
              />
            </CardBody>

            <CardFooter>
              <Button
                type="submit"
                className="w-full"
                loading={isPending}
              >
                Log Masuk
              </Button>
            </CardFooter>
          </form>
        </Card>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

```

```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-20 09:59:17Z)**_

All files created, type-check passes, build succeeds. Here is a summary of what was built:

## Files Created (7 new files)

### Server Actions
- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/lib/actions/auth.ts` -- `loginAction` (uses `useActionState` pattern, calls `signIn("credentials")`, returns error state on failure, redirects to `/admin` on success) and `logoutAction` (calls `signOut`, redirects to `/admin/login`).

### Admin Layout
- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/app/admin/layout.tsx` -- Server component that calls `auth()` to get the session. When authenticated, wraps children in the `AdminShell` (sidebar + topbar). When no session (i.e., the login page), renders children directly without the shell. This avoids the need for route groups while keeping the login page standalone.

### Login Page
- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/app/admin/login/layout.tsx` -- Minimal pass-through layout with login-specific metadata.
- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/app/admin/login/page.tsx` -- Client component using `useActionState` with `loginAction`. Email + password form in a centered card on cream background. Shows inline error "E-mel atau kata laluan salah" on failure. Uses existing `Button`, `Input`, and `Card` UI components.

### Admin Shell Components
- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/components/admin/admin-shell.tsx` -- Client component that manages sidebar open/close state and composes `Sidebar` + `Topbar` + main content area in a flex layout.
- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/components/admin/sidebar.tsx` -- Client component with `usePathname()` for active nav highlighting. 7 nav items (Dashboard, Slot Temujanji, Temujanji, Slot Tempahan, Tempahan, Pakej, Pelanggan) with Heroicons-style SVG icons. Navy background, responsive: hidden on mobile with overlay toggle, full-width on desktop. Logout button at bottom.
- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/components/admin/topbar.tsx` -- Shows admin name/email, mobile hamburger toggle, and logout button. White background with navy text.

### Dashboard Placeholder
- `/home/quantmis/projects/mawarbiru/app/admin/page.tsx` -- Simple heading "Dashboard" with welcome text, ready for 6.2 to fill in.

### Key Design Decisions
- The admin layout conditionally renders the shell based on session presence (no session = pass-through for login page), working harmoniously with the existing middleware that allows `/admin/login` without auth.
- Used `useActionState` (React 19) for the login form instead of manual state management -- the Next.js 16 recommended pattern.
- All components use the project's brand colors (navy sidebar, cream login background, white content area) and existing UI primitives.
